1# -*- coding: utf-8 -*- 

2# pylint: disable=wrong-import-position, import-outside-toplevel, no-name-in-module 

3"""Location: ./mcpgateway/main.py 

4Copyright 2026 

5SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0 

6Authors: Mihai Criveti, Eleni Kechrioti 

7 

8ContextForge AI Gateway - Main FastAPI Application. 

9 

10This module defines the core FastAPI application for the Model Context Protocol (MCP) Gateway. 

11It serves as the entry point for handling all HTTP and WebSocket traffic. 

12 

13Features and Responsibilities: 

14- Initializes and orchestrates services for tools, resources, prompts, servers, gateways, and roots. 

15- Supports full MCP protocol operations: initialize, ping, notify, complete, and sample. 

16- Integrates authentication (JWT and basic), CORS, caching, and middleware. 

17- Serves a rich Admin UI for managing gateway entities via HTMX-based frontend. 

18- Exposes routes for JSON-RPC, SSE, and WebSocket transports. 

19- Manages application lifecycle including startup and graceful shutdown of all services. 

20 

21Structure: 

22- Declares routers for MCP protocol operations and administration. 

23- Registers dependencies (e.g., DB sessions, auth handlers). 

24- Applies middleware including custom documentation protection. 

25- Configures resource caching and session registry using pluggable backends. 

26- Provides OpenAPI metadata and redirect handling depending on UI feature flags. 

27""" 

28

216# Initialize logging service first 

217logging_service = LoggingService() 

218logger = logging_service.get_logger("mcpgateway") 

219 

220# Note: Logging configuration is handled by LoggingService during startup 

221# Don't use basicConfig here as it conflicts with our dual logging setup 

222# Note: DB readiness probing and bootstrap_db() are deferred to the lifespan 

223# startup hook so that `import mcpgateway.main` does no I/O. See lifespan(). 

224 

225# Enable plugin subsystem at module load time, mirroring the old singleton pattern. 

226# get_plugin_manager() guards on this flag, so it must be set before lifespan runs. 

227if settings.plugins.enabled: 

228 enable_plugins(True) 

229 logger.info("Plugin subsystem enabled (factory will be initialized in lifespan)") 

230 

231# First-Party

242sampling_handler = SamplingHandler() 

243tag_service = TagService() 

244export_service = ExportService() 

245import_service = ImportService()

248

251

254 # First-Party 

255 from mcpgateway.utils.redis_client import _build_ssl_kwargs 

256 

257 wait_for_redis_ready( 

258 redis_url=settings.redis_url, 

259 max_retries=int(settings.redis_max_retries), 

260 retry_interval_ms=int(settings.redis_retry_interval_ms), 

261 ssl_kwargs=_build_ssl_kwargs(settings), 

262 sync=True, 

263 ) 

264 

265# Initialize session registry 

266session_registry = SessionRegistry( 

267 backend=settings.cache_type, 

268 redis_url=settings.redis_url if settings.cache_type == "redis" else None, 

269 database_url=settings.database_url if settings.cache_type == "database" else None, 

270 session_ttl=settings.session_ttl, 

271 message_ttl=settings.message_ttl, 

272) 

273set_shared_session_registry(session_registry) 

274 

275 

276_INTERNAL_MCP_AUTH_CONTEXT_HEADER = "x-contextforge-auth-context" 

277 

278 

279def _is_trusted_internal_mcp_runtime_request(request: Request) -> bool: 

280 """Return whether the request came from the local Rust runtime sidecar. 

281 

282 Args: 

283 request: Incoming request to inspect. 

284 

285 Returns: 

286 ``True`` when the request carries the trusted Rust runtime marker from 

287 loopback, otherwise ``False``. 

288 """ 

289 runtime_marker = request.headers.get("x-contextforge-mcp-runtime") 

290 client_host = getattr(getattr(request, "client", None), "host", None) 

291 if runtime_marker != "rust" or not has_valid_internal_mcp_runtime_auth_header(request) or client_host not in ("127.0.0.1", "::1"): 

292 return False 

293 # Defense-in-depth: /_internal/a2a/* endpoints must refuse requests when 

294 # A2A support is disabled, even from an otherwise-trusted local sidecar. 

295 # A legitimate sidecar should not be running when the feature is off. 

296 path = getattr(getattr(request, "url", None), "path", "") or "" 

297 if path.startswith("/_internal/a2a/") and not settings.mcpgateway_a2a_enabled: 

298 return False 

299 return True 

300 

301 

302def _is_jwt_token(token: str) -> bool: 

303 """Check if a token looks like a JWT (has 2 dots, 3 base64url parts). 

304 

305 Rejects local opaque tokens (cf_sess_*, cf_pat_*) that remote gateways 

306 cannot validate. 

307 """ 

308 if not token: 

309 return False 

310 if token.startswith(("cf_sess_", "cf_pat_")): 

311 return False 

312 parts = token.split(".") 

313 if len(parts) != 3: 

314 return False 

315 

316 for part in parts: 

317 if not part: 

318 return False 

319 try: 

320 padded = part + "=" * (-len(part) % 4) 

321 base64.urlsafe_b64decode(padded) 

322 except Exception: # pylint: disable=broad-exception-caught 

323 return False 

324 return True 

325 

326 

327def _build_internal_mcp_forwarded_user(request: Request) -> Dict[str, Any]: 

328 """Build the authenticated user payload for internal Rust -> Python MCP dispatch. 

329 

330 Args: 

331 request: Trusted internal request forwarded from the Rust runtime. 

332 

333 Returns: 

334 Synthetic authenticated user payload used by internal MCP handlers. 

335 

336 Raises: 

337 HTTPException: If the request is not trusted or the forwarded auth context 

338 is missing or invalid. 

339 """ 

340 if not _is_trusted_internal_mcp_runtime_request(request): 

341 raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="Internal MCP dispatch is only available to the local Rust runtime") 

342 

343 header_value = request.headers.get(_INTERNAL_MCP_AUTH_CONTEXT_HEADER) 

344 if not header_value: 

345 raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Missing trusted MCP auth context") 

346 

347 try: 

348 auth_context = decode_internal_mcp_auth_context(header_value) 

349 except Exception as exc: 

350 logger.debug("Invalid trusted MCP auth context: %s", exc) 

351 raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Invalid trusted MCP auth context") from exc 

352 

353 setattr(request.state, "_mcp_internal_auth_context", auth_context) 

354 

355 if "teams" in auth_context and (auth_context["teams"] is None or isinstance(auth_context["teams"], list)): 

356 request.state.token_teams = auth_context["teams"] 

357 

358 if request.headers.get(INTERNAL_MCP_SESSION_VALIDATED_HEADER) == "rust": 

359 auth_context["_rust_session_validated"] = True 

360 

361 forwarded_auth_method = auth_context.get("auth_method") or "mcp_internal_forward" 

362 

363 set_trace_context_from_teams( 

364 auth_context.get("teams"), 

365 user_email=auth_context.get("email"), 

366 is_admin=bool(auth_context.get("permission_is_admin", auth_context.get("is_admin", False))), 

367 auth_method=forwarded_auth_method, 

368 team_name=auth_context.get("team_name"), 

369 ) 

370 

371 return { 

372 "email": auth_context.get("email"), 

373 "full_name": auth_context.get("email") or "MCP Internal Forward", 

374 "is_admin": bool(auth_context.get("permission_is_admin", auth_context.get("is_admin", False))), 

375 "auth_method": forwarded_auth_method, 

376 "token_use": auth_context.get("token_use"), 

377 } 

378 

379 

380def _enforce_internal_mcp_server_scope(request: Request, server_id: str) -> None: 

381 """Validate trusted internal server scope against any forwarded token server scope. 

382 

383 Args: 

384 request: Trusted internal MCP request. 

385 server_id: Effective virtual server identifier for the operation. 

386 

387 Raises: 

388 HTTPException: If the forwarded token scope does not authorize the server. 

389 """ 

390 auth_context = get_internal_mcp_auth_context(request) 

391 if not isinstance(auth_context, dict): 

392 return 

393 

394 scoped_server_id = auth_context.get("scoped_server_id") 

395 if isinstance(scoped_server_id, str) and scoped_server_id and not validate_server_access({"server_id": scoped_server_id}, server_id): 

396 raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail=f"Token not authorized for server: {server_id}") 

397 

398 

399async def _authorize_internal_mcp_request(request: Request, db: Session, *, permission: str, method: str, server_id: Optional[str] = None): 

400 """Authorize trusted Rust-side MCP dispatch while preserving permissive MCP semantics. 

401 

402 For authenticated callers, this enforces the same token-scope and RBAC rules as 

403 the regular RPC dispatcher. For unauthenticated MCP callers in permissive mode, 

404 StreamableHTTP middleware already downgraded them to public-only scope and 

405 enforced per-server OAuth, so the internal Rust -> Python hop should not re-deny 

406 public-only requests merely because there is no authenticated RBAC identity. 

407 

408 Args: 

409 request: Trusted internal MCP request. 

410 db: Active database session. 

411 permission: RBAC permission required for the method. 

412 method: MCP method name being authorized. 

413 server_id: Optional virtual server identifier used for additional scope checks. 

414 

415 Returns: 

416 The forwarded user payload used for downstream authorization and scoping. 

417 """ 

418 user = _build_internal_mcp_forwarded_user(request) 

419 auth_context = get_internal_mcp_auth_context(request) or {} 

420 

421 if server_id: 

422 _enforce_internal_mcp_server_scope(request, server_id) 

423 

424 if auth_context.get("is_authenticated", True) is True: 

425 await _ensure_rpc_permission(user, db, permission, method, request=request) 

426 

427 return user 

428 

429 

430def _build_internal_mcp_auth_scope( 

431 *, 

432 method: str, 

433 path: str, 

434 query_string: str, 

435 headers: Dict[str, str], 

436 client_ip: Optional[str], 

437) -> Dict[str, Any]: 

438 """Construct a synthetic ASGI scope for internal Rust -> Python MCP auth. 

439 

440 Args: 

441 method: HTTP method of the original public MCP request. 

442 path: Public MCP path, for example ``/mcp`` or ``/servers/<id>/mcp``. 

443 query_string: Raw query string without the leading ``?``. 

444 headers: Public request headers to replay through auth/token scoping. 

445 client_ip: Effective client IP derived by Rust from the public request. 

446 

447 Returns: 

448 ASGI scope dictionary suitable for token scoping and ``streamable_http_auth``. 

449 """ 

450 raw_headers = [] 

451 for name, value in headers.items(): 

452 if not isinstance(name, str) or not isinstance(value, str): 

453 continue 

454 raw_headers.append((name.lower().encode("latin-1"), value.encode("latin-1"))) 

455 

456 return { 

457 "type": "http", 

458 "method": method.upper(), 

459 "path": path, 

460 "raw_path": path.encode("latin-1"), 

461 "query_string": query_string.encode("latin-1"), 

462 "headers": raw_headers, 

463 "client": (client_ip or "unknown", 0), 

464 "state": {}, 

465 } 

466 

467 

468async def _run_internal_mcp_authentication( 

469 *, 

470 method: str, 

471 path: str, 

472 query_string: str, 

473 headers: Dict[str, str], 

474 client_ip: Optional[str], 

475) -> tuple[Optional[Response], Dict[str, Any]]: 

476 """Run token scoping and MCP transport auth for a direct Rust ingress request. 

477 

478 Runs HTTP_PRE_REQUEST plugin hooks (e.g. WXO auth token exchange) before 

479 authentication so the Rust MCP path gets identical plugin behavior to the 

480 Python middleware chain. 

481 

482 Args: 

483 method: HTTP method of the public request. 

484 path: Public request path. 

485 query_string: Raw query string without the leading ``?``. 

486 headers: Public request headers replayed from Rust. 

487 client_ip: Effective client IP for token-scope IP restriction checks. 

488 

489 Returns: 

490 Tuple of ``(error_response, auth_context)``. 

491 ``error_response`` is ``None`` on success; otherwise it contains the exact 

492 response generated by the existing token-scoping/auth layers. 

493 """ 

494 # Run pre-request plugin hooks (e.g. WXO JWT → team token exchange) 

495 # before building the auth scope, so plugins can transform headers. 

496 plugin_manager = await get_plugin_manager() 

497 if plugin_manager and plugin_manager.has_hooks_for(HttpHookType.HTTP_PRE_REQUEST): 

498 headers, _, _ = await run_pre_request_hooks( 

499 plugin_manager=plugin_manager, 

500 headers=headers, 

501 path=path, 

502 method=method, 

503 client_host=client_ip, 

504 ) 

505 

506 scope = _build_internal_mcp_auth_scope( 

507 method=method, 

508 path=path, 

509 query_string=query_string, 

510 headers=headers, 

511 client_ip=client_ip, 

512 ) 

513 request = starletteRequest(scope) 

514 sent_messages: list[dict[str, Any]] = [] 

515 

516 async def _receive() -> dict[str, Any]: 

517 """Return an empty request body for the synthetic auth probe. 

518 

519 Returns: 

520 Minimal ASGI ``http.request`` message with no body content. 

521 """ 

522 return {"type": "http.request", "body": b"", "more_body": False} 

523 

524 async def _send(message: dict[str, Any]) -> None: 

525 """Capture ASGI response messages emitted by auth middleware. 

526 

527 Args: 

528 message: ASGI response message emitted by the auth stack. 

529 """ 

530 sent_messages.append(message) 

531 

532 def _captured_response() -> Response: 

533 """Build a concrete response from the captured ASGI messages. 

534 

535 Returns: 

536 Response reconstructed from the captured auth middleware output. 

537 """ 

538 status_code = 500 

539 response_headers: Dict[str, str] = {} 

540 body = b"" 

541 for message in sent_messages: 

542 if message.get("type") == "http.response.start": 

543 status_code = int(message.get("status", 500)) 

544 response_headers = { 

545 key.decode("latin-1"): value.decode("latin-1") for key, value in message.get("headers", []) if isinstance(key, (bytes, bytearray)) and isinstance(value, (bytes, bytearray)) 

546 } 

547 elif message.get("type") == "http.response.body": 

548 body += message.get("body", b"") 

549 return Response(content=body, status_code=status_code, headers=response_headers) 

550 

551 async def _call_next(_request: starletteRequest) -> Response: 

552 """Run the existing Streamable HTTP auth layer for the synthetic request. 

553 

554 Returns: 

555 Success response when authentication passes, otherwise the captured 

556 failure response emitted by the existing middleware chain. 

557 """ 

558 auth_ok = await streamable_http_auth(scope, _receive, _send) 

559 if auth_ok: 

560 return ORJSONResponse(status_code=200, content={"authenticated": True}) 

561 return _captured_response() 

562 

563 original_context = user_context_var.get() 

564 user_context_var.set({}) 

565 try: 

566 if settings.email_auth_enabled: 

567 response = await token_scoping_middleware(request, _call_next) 

568 else: 

569 response = await _call_next(request) 

570 

571 if response is None: 

572 response = _captured_response() 

573 

574 if response.status_code >= 400: 

575 return response, {} 

576 

577 return None, get_streamable_http_auth_context() 

578 finally: 

579 user_context_var.set(original_context) 

580 

581 

582def _normalize_token_teams(teams: Optional[List]) -> List[str]: 

583 """ 

584 Normalize token teams to list of team IDs. 

585 

586 SSO tokens may contain team dicts like {"id": "...", "name": "..."}. 

587 This normalizes to just IDs for consistent filtering. 

588 

589 Args: 

590 teams: Raw teams from token payload (may be None, list of IDs, or list of dicts) 

591 

592 Returns: 

593 List of team ID strings (empty list if None) 

594 

595 Examples: 

596 >>> from mcpgateway import main 

597 >>> main._normalize_token_teams(None) 

598 [] 

599 >>> main._normalize_token_teams([]) 

600 [] 

601 >>> main._normalize_token_teams(["team_a", "team_b"]) 

602 ['team_a', 'team_b'] 

603 >>> main._normalize_token_teams([{"id": "team_a", "name": "Team A"}]) 

604 ['team_a'] 

605 >>> main._normalize_token_teams([{"id": "t1"}, "t2", {"name": "no_id"}]) 

606 ['t1', 't2'] 

607 """ 

608 if not teams: 

609 return [] 

610 

611 normalized = [] 

612 for team in teams: 

613 if isinstance(team, dict): 

614 team_id = team.get("id") 

615 if team_id: 

616 normalized.append(team_id) 

617 elif isinstance(team, str): 

618 normalized.append(team) 

619 return normalized 

620 

621 

622def _build_rpc_permission_user(user, db: Session) -> dict[str, Any]: 

623 """Build PermissionChecker user payload for method-level RPC checks. 

624 

625 Args: 

626 user: Authenticated user context. 

627 db: Active database session. 

628 

629 Returns: 

630 Permission checker payload with email and ``db`` keys. 

631 """ 

632 permission_user = dict(user) if isinstance(user, dict) else {"email": get_user_email(user)} 

633 if not permission_user.get("email"): 

634 permission_user["email"] = get_user_email(user) 

635 permission_user["db"] = db 

636 return permission_user 

637 

638 

639def _extract_scoped_permissions(request: Request) -> set[str] | None: 

640 """Extract token scopes.permissions from cached JWT payload. 

641 

642 Args: 

643 request: Incoming request context. 

644 

645 Returns: 

646 None: no explicit scope cap (empty permissions or no JWT — defer to RBAC) 

647 set: explicit permission set (may contain '*' for wildcard) 

648 """ 

649 internal_auth_context = get_internal_mcp_auth_context(request) 

650 if isinstance(internal_auth_context, dict): 

651 permissions = internal_auth_context.get("scoped_permissions") 

652 if not permissions: 

653 return None 

654 return set(permissions) 

655 

656 cached = getattr(request.state, "_jwt_verified_payload", None) 

657 if not cached or not isinstance(cached, tuple) or len(cached) != 2: 

658 return None 

659 _, payload = cached 

660 if not payload or not isinstance(payload, dict): 

661 return None 

662 scopes = payload.get("scopes") 

663 if not scopes or not isinstance(scopes, dict): 

664 return None 

665 permissions = scopes.get("permissions") 

666 if not permissions: # Empty list or None = defer to RBAC 

667 return None 

668 return set(permissions) 

669 

670 

671def _is_permission_admin_user(user) -> bool: 

672 """Return whether the caller already has permission-layer admin authority. 

673 

674 This is stricter than token-scope admin semantics. It is used only to skip 

675 redundant RBAC DB lookups after token scope caps have already been enforced. 

676 

677 Args: 

678 user: Authenticated user object or dict-like payload. 

679 

680 Returns: 

681 ``True`` when the caller already has permission-layer admin authority. 

682 """ 

683 if hasattr(user, "is_admin"): 

684 return bool(getattr(user, "is_admin", False)) 

685 if isinstance(user, dict): 

686 if "permission_is_admin" in user: 

687 return bool(user.get("permission_is_admin", False)) 

688 return False 

689 return False 

690 

691 

692async def _ensure_rpc_permission(user, db: Session, permission: str, method: str, request: Request | None = None) -> None: 

693 """Require a specific RPC permission for a method branch. 

694 

695 Enforces both layers: 

696 1. Token scopes.permissions cap (if explicit permissions present) 

697 2. RBAC role-based permission check 

698 

699 Args: 

700 user: Authenticated user context. 

701 db: Active database session. 

702 permission: Permission required for the method. 

703 method: JSON-RPC method name being authorized. 

704 request: Optional FastAPI request for extracting token scopes. 

705 

706 Raises: 

707 JSONRPCError: If the requester lacks the required permission. 

708 """ 

709 # Layer 1: Token scope cap 

710 if request is not None: 

711 scoped = _extract_scoped_permissions(request) 

712 if scoped is not None and "*" not in scoped and permission not in scoped: 

713 logger.warning("RPC permission denied (token scope): method=%s, required=%s", method, permission) 

714 raise JSONRPCError(-32003, _ACCESS_DENIED_MSG, {"method": method}) 

715 

716 if permission == "admin.system_config" and _is_permission_admin_user(user): 

717 return 

718 

719 # Layer 2: RBAC check 

720 # /rpc payloads never carry a resource with an owning team, so we skip 

721 # resource/payload derivation (unlike @require_permission). For single- 

722 # team API tokens we extract team_id from the token itself; otherwise 

723 # fall back to check_any_team so team-scoped roles are found. 

724 # Layer 1 (token scope cap above) already restricts visibility. 

725 team_id: str | None = None 

726 check_any_team = False 

727 if isinstance(user, dict): 

728 team_id = user.get("team_id") 

729 if not team_id: 

730 check_any_team = True 

731 checker = PermissionChecker(_build_rpc_permission_user(user, db)) 

732 if not await checker.has_permission(permission, check_any_team=check_any_team, team_id=team_id): 

733 logger.warning("RPC permission denied (RBAC): method=%s, required=%s", method, permission) 

734 raise JSONRPCError(-32003, _ACCESS_DENIED_MSG, {"method": method}) 

735 

736 

737def _serialize_mcp_tool_definition(tool: Any) -> Dict[str, Any]: 

738 """Return an MCP-compliant tool definition without API-only metadata fields. 

739 

740 Args: 

741 tool: Tool ORM object, pydantic model, or dict-like payload. 

742 

743 Returns: 

744 MCP-compatible tool definition dictionary. 

745 """ 

746 if hasattr(tool, "model_dump"): 

747 data = tool.model_dump(by_alias=True, exclude_none=True) 

748 elif isinstance(tool, dict): 

749 data = dict(tool) 

750 else: 

751 data = {} 

752 

753 name = data.get("name", getattr(tool, "name", None)) 

754 description = data.get("description", getattr(tool, "description", None)) 

755 input_schema = data.get("inputSchema", getattr(tool, "input_schema", None)) 

756 

757 payload: Dict[str, Any] = {} 

758 if name is not None: 

759 payload["name"] = name 

760 if description is not None or name is not None or input_schema is not None: 

761 payload["description"] = description or "" 

762 if input_schema is not None: 

763 payload["inputSchema"] = input_schema 

764 

765 output_schema = data.get("outputSchema", getattr(tool, "output_schema", None)) 

766 if output_schema is not None: 

767 payload["outputSchema"] = output_schema 

768 

769 annotations = data.get("annotations", getattr(tool, "annotations", None)) 

770 if annotations is not None: 

771 payload["annotations"] = annotations 

772 

773 return {key: value for key, value in payload.items() if value is not None} 

774 

775 

776def _serialize_mcp_tool_definitions(tools: List[Any]) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]: 

777 """Serialize tool records to MCP tool definitions. 

778 

779 Args: 

780 tools: Iterable of tool-like records to serialize. 

781 

782 Returns: 

783 List of MCP-compatible tool definitions. 

784 """ 

785 return [_serialize_mcp_tool_definition(tool) for tool in tools] 

786 

787 

788def _serialize_legacy_tool_payloads(tools: List[Any]) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]: 

789 """Serialize tool records using the legacy JSON-RPC shape. 

790 

791 Args: 

792 tools: Iterable of tool-like records to serialize. 

793 

794 Returns: 

795 List of legacy tool payload dictionaries. 

796 """ 

797 payloads: List[Dict[str, Any]] = [] 

798 for tool in tools: 

799 if hasattr(tool, "model_dump"): 

800 payload = tool.model_dump(by_alias=True, exclude_none=True) 

801 elif isinstance(tool, dict): 

802 payload = dict(tool) 

803 else: 

804 payload = {} 

805 payloads.append(payload) 

806 return payloads 

807 

808 

809def _enforce_scoped_resource_access(request: Request, db: Session, user, resource_path: str) -> None: 

810 """Apply token-scope ownership checks for a concrete resource path. 

811 

812 This provides defense-in-depth for ID-based handlers so they continue to 

813 enforce visibility even if middleware coverage regresses. 

814 

815 Args: 

816 request: Incoming request context. 

817 db: Active database session. 

818 user: Authenticated user context. 

819 resource_path: Canonical resource path (e.g. ``/tools/{id}``). 

820 

821 Raises: 

822 HTTPException: If access to the target resource is not allowed. 

823 """ 

824 scoped_user_email, scoped_token_teams = get_scoped_resource_access_context(request, user) 

825 

826 # Admin bypass / unrestricted scope 

827 if scoped_token_teams is None: 

828 return 

829 

830 if not token_scoping_middleware._check_resource_team_ownership( # pylint: disable=protected-access 

831 resource_path, 

832 scoped_token_teams, 

833 db=db, 

834 _user_email=scoped_user_email, 

835 ): 

836 logger.warning("Scoped resource access denied: user=%s, resource=%s", scoped_user_email, resource_path) 

837 raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN, detail=_ACCESS_DENIED_MSG) 

838 

839 

840async def _assert_session_owner_or_admin(request: Request, user, session_id: str) -> None: 

841 """Ensure session operations are limited to the owner unless requester is admin. 

842 

843 Args: 

844 request: Incoming request context. 

845 user: Authenticated user context. 

846 session_id: Target session identifier. 

847 

848 Raises: 

849 HTTPException: If session is missing or requester is not authorized. 

850 """ 

851 session_owner = await session_registry.get_session_owner(session_id) 

852 if not session_owner: 

853 session_exists = await session_registry.session_exists(session_id) 

854 if session_exists is False: 

855 raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Session not found") 

856 raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="Session owner metadata unavailable") 

857 

858 requester_email, requester_is_admin = get_request_identity(request, user) 

859 if requester_is_admin: 

860 return 

861 if requester_email and requester_email == session_owner: 

862 return 

863 raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="Session access denied") 

864 

865 

866async def _authorize_run_cancellation(request: Request, user, request_id: str, *, as_jsonrpc_error: bool) -> None: 

867 """Authorize a notifications/cancelled request for a specific run id. 

868 

869 Args: 

870 request: Incoming request context. 

871 user: Authenticated user context. 

872 request_id: Run/request identifier to cancel. 

873 as_jsonrpc_error: Raise ``JSONRPCError`` when True, otherwise ``HTTPException``. 

874 

875 Raises: 

876 JSONRPCError: When ``as_jsonrpc_error`` is True and cancellation is not authorized. 

877 HTTPException: When ``as_jsonrpc_error`` is False and cancellation is not authorized. 

878 """ 

879 requester_email, requester_token_teams, requester_is_admin = get_rpc_filter_context(request, user) 

880 requester_teams = [] if requester_token_teams is None else list(requester_token_teams) 

881 run_status = await cancellation_service.get_status(request_id) 

882 

883 if run_status is None: 

884 # Notifications are best-effort; unknown request ids should be accepted 

885 # as no-ops rather than rejected as authorization failures. 

886 return 

887 

888 run_owner_email = run_status.get("owner_email") 

889 run_owner_team_ids = run_status.get("owner_team_ids") or [] 

890 requester_is_owner = bool(run_owner_email and requester_email and run_owner_email == requester_email) 

891 requester_shares_team = bool(run_owner_team_ids and requester_teams and any(team in run_owner_team_ids for team in requester_teams)) 

892 unauthorized = not requester_is_admin and not requester_is_owner and not requester_shares_team 

893 

894 if unauthorized: 

895 if as_jsonrpc_error: 

896 raise JSONRPCError(-32003, "Not authorized to cancel this run", {"requestId": request_id}) 

897 raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN, detail="Not authorized to cancel this run") 

898 

899 

900# Initialize cache 

901resource_cache = ResourceCache(max_size=settings.resource_cache_size, ttl=settings.resource_cache_ttl) 

902 

903 

904def _rust_build_included() -> bool: 

905 """Return whether the current image includes Rust MCP artifacts. 

906 

907 Returns: 

908 ``True`` when the current image contains the Rust MCP binaries/plugins. 

909 """ 

910 return version_module.rust_build_included() 

911 

912 

913def _rust_runtime_managed() -> bool: 

914 """Return whether the gateway expects to manage the Rust MCP sidecar locally. 

915 

916 Returns: 

917 ``True`` when the gateway should launch and supervise the Rust sidecar. 

918 """ 

919 return version_module.rust_runtime_managed() 

920 

921 

922def _current_mcp_transport_mount() -> str: 

923 """Return which public /mcp transport is currently mounted. 

924 

925 Returns: 

926 Runtime label identifying the currently mounted public MCP transport. 

927 """ 

928 return version_module.current_mcp_transport_mount() 

929 

930 

931def _should_mount_public_rust_transport() -> bool: 

932 """Return whether the public ``/mcp`` path should be served directly by Rust. 

933 

934 Returns: 

935 ``True`` only when the Rust runtime is enabled and the session-auth reuse 

936 path is enabled, allowing Rust to safely own steady-state public MCP 

937 session traffic. Otherwise returns ``False`` and leaves public MCP on 

938 the Python ingress path. 

939 """ 

940 return version_module.should_mount_public_rust_transport() 

941 

942 

943def _should_use_rust_public_session_stack() -> bool: 

944 """Return whether Rust should own the effective public MCP session stack. 

945 

946 Returns: 

947 ``True`` only when the Rust runtime is enabled and session-auth reuse is 

948 enabled, allowing the public transport, session metadata, replay/resume, 

949 live-stream, and affinity behavior to stay on a consistent Rust-backed 

950 path. Otherwise returns ``False`` so the public MCP session stack falls 

951 back to Python semantics. 

952 """ 

953 return version_module.should_use_rust_public_session_stack() 

954 

955 

956def _current_mcp_runtime_mode() -> str: 

957 """Return a compact runtime-mode label for observability. 

958 

959 Returns: 

960 Human-readable runtime mode label for health/readiness reporting. 

961 """ 

962 return version_module.current_mcp_runtime_mode() 

963 

964 

965def _current_mcp_session_core_mode() -> str: 

966 """Return which session core currently owns MCP session metadata. 

967 

968 Returns: 

969 ``"rust"`` when the Rust session core is enabled, otherwise ``"python"``. 

970 """ 

971 return version_module.current_mcp_session_core_mode() 

972 

973 

974def _current_mcp_event_store_mode() -> str: 

975 """Return which runtime currently owns MCP resumable event-store semantics. 

976 

977 Returns: 

978 ``"rust"`` when the Rust event store is enabled, otherwise ``"python"``. 

979 """ 

980 return version_module.current_mcp_event_store_mode() 

981 

982 

983def _current_mcp_resume_core_mode() -> str: 

984 """Return which runtime currently owns public MCP replay/resume behavior. 

985 

986 Returns: 

987 ``"rust"`` when Rust owns replay/resume, otherwise ``"python"``. 

988 """ 

989 return version_module.current_mcp_resume_core_mode() 

990 

991 

992def _current_mcp_live_stream_core_mode() -> str: 

993 """Return which runtime currently owns non-resume public GET /mcp SSE behavior. 

994 

995 Returns: 

996 ``"rust"`` when Rust owns live GET /mcp streaming, otherwise ``"python"``. 

997 """ 

998 return version_module.current_mcp_live_stream_core_mode() 

999 

1000 

1001def _current_mcp_affinity_core_mode() -> str: 

1002 """Return which runtime currently owns MCP multi-worker session-affinity forwarding. 

1003 

1004 Returns: 

1005 ``"rust"`` when Rust owns session-affinity forwarding, otherwise ``"python"``. 

1006 """ 

1007 return version_module.current_mcp_affinity_core_mode() 

1008 

1009 

1010def _current_mcp_session_auth_reuse_mode() -> str: 

1011 """Return which runtime currently owns MCP session-bound auth-context reuse. 

1012 

1013 Returns: 

1014 ``"rust"`` when Rust session auth reuse is enabled, otherwise ``"python"``. 

1015 """ 

1016 return version_module.current_mcp_session_auth_reuse_mode() 

1017 

1018 

1019def _mcp_runtime_status_payload() -> Dict[str, Any]: 

1020 """Return MCP runtime diagnostics for health/readiness endpoints. 

1021 

1022 Returns: 

1023 Diagnostic payload describing the active MCP runtime configuration. 

1024 """ 

1025 return version_module.mcp_runtime_status_payload() 

1026 

1027 

1028def _apply_runtime_mode_headers(response: Response) -> None: 

1029 """Attach MCP runtime mode headers to a response. 

1030 

1031 Args: 

1032 response: Response object to annotate. 

1033 """ 

1034 response.headers["x-contextforge-mcp-runtime-mode"] = _current_mcp_runtime_mode() 

1035 response.headers["x-contextforge-mcp-transport-mounted"] = _current_mcp_transport_mount() 

1036 response.headers["x-contextforge-rust-build-included"] = "true" if _rust_build_included() else "false" 

1037 response.headers["x-contextforge-mcp-session-core-mode"] = _current_mcp_session_core_mode() 

1038 response.headers["x-contextforge-mcp-event-store-mode"] = _current_mcp_event_store_mode() 

1039 response.headers["x-contextforge-mcp-resume-core-mode"] = _current_mcp_resume_core_mode() 

1040 response.headers["x-contextforge-mcp-live-stream-core-mode"] = _current_mcp_live_stream_core_mode() 

1041 response.headers["x-contextforge-mcp-affinity-core-mode"] = _current_mcp_affinity_core_mode() 

1042 response.headers["x-contextforge-mcp-session-auth-reuse-mode"] = _current_mcp_session_auth_reuse_mode() 

1043 

1044 

1045# Type aliases for improved readability 

1046ToolsResponse: TypeAlias = Union[List[ToolRead], CursorPaginatedToolsResponse, List[Dict[Any, Any]], Dict[Any, Any], ORJSONResponse] 

1047ToolResponse: TypeAlias = Union[ToolRead, Dict[Any, Any], ORJSONResponse] 

1048 

1049 

1050@lru_cache(maxsize=512) 

1051def _parse_jsonpath(jsonpath: str) -> JSONPath: 

1052 """Cache parsed JSONPath expression. 

1053 

1054 Args: 

1055 jsonpath: The JSONPath expression string. 

1056 

1057 Returns: 

1058 Parsed JSONPath object. 

1059 

1060 Raises: 

1061 Exception: If the JSONPath expression is invalid. 

1062 """ 

1063 return parse(jsonpath) 

1064 

1065 

1066def _parse_apijsonpath(raw: Optional[Union[str, JsonPathModifier]]) -> Optional[JsonPathModifier]: 

1067 """ 

1068 Parse apijsonpath parameter from either a JSON string or a JsonPathModifier model. 

1069 

1070 Performs early validation of JSONPath syntax to fail fast and provide clear error messages. 

1071 

1072 Args: 

1073 raw: Either a JSON-encoded string or a JsonPathModifier instance 

1074 

1075 Returns: 

1076 Parsed JsonPathModifier or None if raw is None 

1077 

1078 Raises: 

1079 HTTPException: If the JSON string is invalid, unexpected type provided, 

1080 jsonpath expression is empty, or JSONPath syntax is invalid (400 Bad Request) 

1081 """ 

1082 if raw is None: 

1083 return None 

1084 

1085 if isinstance(raw, str): 

1086 try: 

1087 parsed = JsonPathModifier.model_validate(json.loads(raw)) 

1088 # Validate jsonpath is not empty if provided 

1089 if parsed.jsonpath is not None: 

1090 if not parsed.jsonpath.strip(): 

1091 raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST, detail="JSONPath expression cannot be empty") 

1092 # Early validation: ensure JSONPath syntax is valid 

1093 try: 

1094 _parse_jsonpath(parsed.jsonpath) 

1095 except Exception as parse_ex: 

1096 detail = f"Invalid JSONPath syntax: {parse_ex}" if settings.log_level == "DEBUG" else "Invalid JSONPath expression" 

1097 raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST, detail=detail) 

1098 return parsed 

1099 except HTTPException: 

1100 # Re-raise HTTPException as-is (includes empty jsonpath and syntax validation) 

1101 raise 

1102 except json.JSONDecodeError as ex: 

1103 # User error: malformed JSON (JSONDecodeError is subclass of ValueError, so catch it specifically) 

1104 detail = f"Invalid apijsonpath JSON: {ex}" if settings.log_level == "DEBUG" else "Invalid apijsonpath format" 

1105 raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST, detail=detail) 

1106 except ValidationError as ex: 

1107 # Pydantic validation error 

1108 detail = f"Invalid apijsonpath structure: {ex}" if settings.log_level == "DEBUG" else "Invalid apijsonpath structure" 

1109 raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST, detail=detail) 

1110 except Exception as ex: 

1111 # Unexpected error - log it and return generic message 

1112 logger.error(f"Unexpected error parsing apijsonpath: {ex}", exc_info=True) 

1113 raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_500_INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, detail="Failed to parse apijsonpath") 

1114 elif isinstance(raw, JsonPathModifier): 

1115 # Validate jsonpath is not empty if provided 

1116 if raw.jsonpath is not None: 

1117 if not raw.jsonpath.strip(): 

1118 raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST, detail="JSONPath expression cannot be empty") 

1119 # Early validation: ensure JSONPath syntax is valid 

1120 try: 

1121 _parse_jsonpath(raw.jsonpath) 

1122 except Exception as parse_ex: 

1123 detail = f"Invalid JSONPath syntax: {parse_ex}" if settings.log_level == "DEBUG" else "Invalid JSONPath expression" 

1124 raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST, detail=detail) 

1125 return raw 

1126 

1127 # Unexpected type - fail fast with clear error message 

1128 # Only show type name in debug mode to avoid information disclosure 

1129 type_info = f": got {type(raw).__name__}" if settings.log_level == "DEBUG" else "" 

1130 raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST, detail=f"Invalid apijsonpath type{type_info}") 

1131 

1132 

1133def jsonpath_modifier(data: Any, jsonpath: str = "$[*]", mappings: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None) -> Union[List, Dict]: 

1134 """ 

1135 Applies the given JSONPath expression and mappings to the data. 

1136 Uses cached parsed expressions for performance. 

1137 

1138 Args: 

1139 data: The JSON data to query. 

1140 jsonpath: The JSONPath expression to apply. 

1141 mappings: Optional dictionary of mappings where keys are new field names 

1142 and values are JSONPath expressions. 

1143 

1144 Returns: 

1145 Union[List, Dict]: A list (or mapped list) or a Dict of extracted data. 

1146 

1147 Raises: 

1148 HTTPException: If there's an error parsing or executing the JSONPath expressions. 

1149 

1150 Examples: 

1151 >>> jsonpath_modifier({'a': 1, 'b': 2}, '$.a') 

1152 [1] 

1153 >>> jsonpath_modifier([{'a': 1}, {'a': 2}], '$[*].a') 

1154 [1, 2] 

1155 >>> jsonpath_modifier({'a': {'b': 2}}, '$.a.b') 

1156 [2] 

1157 >>> jsonpath_modifier({'a': 1}, '$.b') 

1158 [] 

1159 """ 

1160 if not jsonpath: 

1161 jsonpath = "$[*]" 

1162 

1163 # Log jsonpath_modifier invocation with structured data (only if debug enabled) 

1164 if logger.isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG): 

1165 data_length = len(data) if isinstance(data, list) else None 

1166 logger.debug(f"jsonpath_modifier: path='{SecurityValidator.sanitize_log_message(jsonpath)}', has_mappings={mappings is not None}, data_type={type(data).__name__}, data_length={data_length}") 

1167 

1168 try: 

1169 main_expr: JSONPath = _parse_jsonpath(jsonpath) 

1170 except Exception as e: 

1171 logger.debug("Invalid main JSONPath expression: %s", e) 

1172 raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Invalid JSONPath expression") 

1173 

1174 try: 

1175 main_matches = main_expr.find(data) 

1176 except Exception as e: 

1177 logger.debug("Error executing main JSONPath: %s", e) 

1178 raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Error executing JSONPath expression") 

1179 

1180 results = [match.value for match in main_matches] 

1181 

1182 if mappings: 

1183 results = transform_data_with_mappings(results, mappings) 

1184 

1185 if len(results) == 1 and isinstance(results[0], dict): 

1186 return results[0] 

1187 

1188 return results 

1189 

1190 

1191def transform_data_with_mappings(data: list[Any], mappings: dict[str, str]) -> list[Any]: 

1192 """ 

1193 Applies mappings to data using cached JSONPath expressions. 

1194 Parses each mapping expression once per call, not per item. 

1195 

1196 Args: 

1197 data: The set of data to apply mappings to. 

1198 mappings: dictionary of mappings where keys are new field names 

1199 

1200 Returns: 

1201 list[Any]: A list (or mapped list) of re-mapped data 

1202 

1203 Raises: 

1204 HTTPException: If there's an error parsing or executing the JSONPath expressions. 

1205 

1206 Examples: 

1207 >>> transform_data_with_mappings([{'first_name': "Bruce", 'second_name': "Wayne"},{'first_name': "Diana", 'second_name': "Prince"}], {"n": "$.first_name"}) 

1208 [{'n': 'Bruce'}, {'n': 'Diana'}] 

1209 """ 

1210 # Pre-parse all mapping expressions once (not per item) 

1211 parsed_mappings: Dict[str, JSONPath] = {} 

1212 for new_key, mapping_expr_str in mappings.items(): 

1213 try: 

1214 parsed_mappings[new_key] = _parse_jsonpath(mapping_expr_str) 

1215 except Exception as e: 

1216 logger.debug("Invalid mapping JSONPath for key '%s': %s", new_key, e) 

1217 raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=f"Invalid JSONPath expression for key '{new_key}'") 

1218 

1219 mapped_results = [] 

1220 for item in data: 

1221 mapped_item = {} 

1222 for new_key, mapping_expr in parsed_mappings.items(): 

1223 try: 

1224 mapping_matches = mapping_expr.find(item) 

1225 except Exception as e: 

1226 logger.debug("Error executing mapping JSONPath for key '%s': %s", new_key, e) 

1227 raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=f"Error executing JSONPath expression for key '{new_key}'") 

1228 

1229 if not mapping_matches: 

1230 mapped_item[new_key] = None 

1231 elif len(mapping_matches) == 1: 

1232 mapped_item[new_key] = mapping_matches[0].value 

1233 else: 

1234 mapped_item[new_key] = [m.value for m in mapping_matches] 

1235 mapped_results.append(mapped_item) 

1236 

1237 return mapped_results 

1238 

1239 

1240async def attempt_to_bootstrap_sso_providers(): 

1241 """ 

1242 Try to bootstrap SSO provider services based on settings. 

1243 """ 

1244 try: 

1245 # First-Party 

1246 from mcpgateway.utils.sso_bootstrap import bootstrap_sso_providers # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

1247 

1248 await bootstrap_sso_providers() 

1249 logger.info("SSO providers bootstrapped successfully") 

1250 except Exception as e: 

1251 logger.warning(f"Failed to bootstrap SSO providers: {e}") 

1252 

1253 

1254#################### 

1255# Startup/Shutdown # 

1256#################### 

1257def _can_manage_sighup_handler() -> bool: 

1258 """Return whether this runtime context can safely install process signal handlers. 

1259 

1260 Returns: 

1261 ``True`` when startup is running on the process main thread and SIGHUP is available. 

1262 """ 

1263 return hasattr(signal, "SIGHUP") and threading.current_thread() is threading.main_thread() 

1264 

1265 

1266def _install_sighup_handler() -> bool: 

1267 """Install the SIGHUP handler when the current runtime context supports it. 

1268 

1269 Returns: 

1270 ``True`` when the handler was installed in the current runtime context. 

1271 """ 

1272 if not _can_manage_sighup_handler(): 

1273 logger.debug("Skipping SIGHUP handler registration outside the main thread") 

1274 return False 

1275 

1276 # First-Party 

1277 from mcpgateway.handlers.signal_handlers import sighup_handler # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

1278 

1279 signal.signal(signal.SIGHUP, sighup_handler) 

1280 return True 

1281 

1282 

1283def _restore_default_sighup_handler() -> None: 

1284 """Restore the default SIGHUP handler when the current runtime context supports it. 

1285 

1286 Returns: 

1287 ``None``. 

1288 """ 

1289 if not _can_manage_sighup_handler(): 

1290 return 

1291 signal.signal(signal.SIGHUP, signal.SIG_DFL) 

1292 

1293 

1294@asynccontextmanager 

1295async def lifespan(_app: FastAPI) -> AsyncIterator[None]: 

1296 """ 

1297 Manage the application's startup and shutdown lifecycle. 

1298 

1299 The function initialises every core service on entry and then 

1300 shuts them down in reverse order on exit. 

1301 

1302 Args: 

1303 _app (FastAPI): FastAPI app 

1304 

1305 Yields: 

1306 None 

1307 

1308 Raises: 

1309 SystemExit: When a critical startup error occurs that prevents 

1310 the application from starting successfully. 

1311 Exception: Any unhandled error that occurs during service 

1312 initialisation or shutdown is re-raised to the caller. 

1313 """ 

1314 aggregation_stop_event: Optional[asyncio.Event] = None 

1315 aggregation_loop_task: Optional[asyncio.Task] = None 

1316 aggregation_backfill_task: Optional[asyncio.Task] = None 

1317 siem_export_service: Optional[Any] = None 

1318 

1319 # Initialize logging service FIRST to ensure all logging goes to dual output 

1320 await logging_service.initialize() 

1321 logger.info("Starting ContextForge services") 

1322 

1323 # Wait for the database to be ready, then run bootstrap (alembic + seed). 

1324 # This used to run at module-import time, which made every test that 

1325 # imported mcpgateway.main pay for a real DB probe and migration check. 

1326 # `wait_for_db_ready(sync=True)` is a blocking probe, so offload it to 

1327 # a worker thread to avoid stalling the event loop during startup. 

1328 # First-Party 

1329 from mcpgateway.bootstrap_db import main as bootstrap_db # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

1330 from mcpgateway.utils.db_isready import wait_for_db_ready # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

1331 

1332 await asyncio.to_thread( 

1333 wait_for_db_ready, 

1334 max_tries=int(settings.db_max_retries), 

1335 interval=int(settings.db_retry_interval_ms) / 1000, 

1336 sync=True, 

1337 ) 

1338 await bootstrap_db() 

1339 

1340 # Initialize Redis client early (shared pool for all services) 

1341 await get_redis_client() 

1342 

1343 # Register the Redis provider with the plugin framework so framework 

1344 # modules can reach Redis without importing mcpgateway.utils directly 

1345 # (isolation enforced by scripts/pre-commit/check_framework_imports.py). 

1346 # First-Party 

1347 from mcpgateway.plugins._redis import set_shared_redis_provider # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

1348 

1349 set_shared_redis_provider(get_redis_client) 

1350 

1351 # Initialize SIEM export service early so security/audit events can flow from startup. 

1352 # First-Party 

1353 from mcpgateway.services.siem_export_service import get_siem_export_service # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

1354 

1355 siem_export_service = get_siem_export_service() 

1356 await siem_export_service.initialize() 

1357 

1358 # Initialize shared HTTP client (connection pool for all outbound requests) 

1359 # First-Party 

1360 from mcpgateway.services.http_client_service import SharedHttpClient # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

1361 

1362 await SharedHttpClient.get_instance() 

1363 

1364 # Update HTTP pool metrics after SharedHttpClient is initialized 

1365 if hasattr(app.state, "update_http_pool_metrics"): 

1366 app.state.update_http_pool_metrics() 

1367 

1368 # Initialize the session-affinity service (Redis-backed worker mapping, 

1369 # heartbeat, session-owner forwarding). Cross-worker upstream 

1370 # ``ClientSession`` state lives in ``UpstreamSessionRegistry`` — 

1371 # SessionAffinity owns only the affinity layer. 

1372 # Always initialize SessionAffinity, regardless of 

1373 # ``mcpgateway_session_affinity_enabled``. The affinity flag controls 

1374 # cross-worker Redis routing; the GET /mcp listener-claim dict 

1375 # (ADR-052) is single-node bookkeeping that lives on the same instance 

1376 # and needs to be a process-wide singleton even with affinity off. 

1377 # Without the always-init, the GET handler would fall back to a fresh 

1378 # ``SessionAffinity()`` per request → each request gets its own 

1379 # ``_listener_claims`` dict → two concurrent GETs both win the claim 

1380 # and the single-listener invariant is broken in the default 

1381 # single-node deployment. 

1382 # 

1383 # The Redis-backed background tasks (heartbeat, RPC listener) are 

1384 # internally gated on the affinity flag, so always-init is safe. 

1385 # First-Party 

1386 from mcpgateway.services.session_affinity import init_session_affinity # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

1387 

1388 # Use enable_notifications=False here so we don't double-init the 

1389 # notification service — main.lifespan does that explicitly below. 

1390 init_session_affinity(enable_notifications=False) 

1391 logger.info( 

1392 "Session-affinity service initialized (affinity_enabled=%s)", 

1393 settings.mcpgateway_session_affinity_enabled, 

1394 ) 

1395 

1396 # Initialize the upstream session registry (#4205). 1:1 binding between a 

1397 # downstream MCP session and its upstream session per gateway, replacing 

1398 # the old identity-keyed sharing semantics. Always on — no feature flag. 

1399 # 

1400 # Wire a notification handler factory so server-initiated messages can be 

1401 # forwarded to GET /mcp listeners (ADR-052). The factory bakes 

1402 # downstream_session_id into the per-session handler closure. 

1403 # First-Party 

1404 from mcpgateway.services.notification_service import init_notification_service # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

1405 from mcpgateway.services.upstream_session_registry import init_upstream_session_registry # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

1406 

1407 _notification_svc = init_notification_service() 

1408 # Initialize the worker before any session is wired through it. 

1409 # Without this, list_changed notifications enqueue into a service 

1410 # whose `_process_refresh_queue` worker never runs and refreshes 

1411 # silently never fire. The gateway_service is wired here 

1412 # unconditionally — the affinity branch's 

1413 # `start_affinity_notification_service` only re-runs under affinity, 

1414 # and without this hand-off the single-node default would leave 

1415 # `_gateway_service is None` and drop every refresh. 

1416 await _notification_svc.initialize(gateway_service=gateway_service) 

1417 

1418 def _notification_handler_factory(url: str, gateway_id, *, downstream_session_id: str): # type: ignore[no-untyped-def] 

1419 """Per-session message handler that routes upstream notifications and forwards server-initiated messages to the GET /mcp listener (ADR-052).""" 

1420 return _notification_svc.create_message_handler( 

1421 gateway_id or url, 

1422 url, 

1423 downstream_session_id=downstream_session_id, 

1424 ) 

1425 

1426 init_upstream_session_registry(message_handler_factory=_notification_handler_factory) 

1427 logger.info("Upstream session registry initialized (notification fanout enabled)") 

1428 

1429 # Initialize LLM chat router Redis client (only if LLM chat is enabled — 

1430 # importing the router pulls in the langchain stack which is several 

1431 # seconds of cold-start cost). 

1432 if settings.llmchat_enabled: 

1433 # First-Party 

1434 from mcpgateway.routers.llmchat_router import init_redis as init_llmchat_redis # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

1435 

1436 await init_llmchat_redis() 

1437 

1438 try: 

1439 # Validate security configuration 

1440 validate_security_configuration() 

1441 

1442 # Validate UAID security configuration 

1443 validate_uaid_security_config() 

1444 

1445 # Initialize the plugin manager factory whenever the YAML config is 

1446 # available. We used to gate this on ``settings.plugins.enabled`` but 

1447 # that broke runtime enable-from-disabled: a node that boots with the 

1448 # flag off would never create the factory, so a later shared-toggle 

1449 # flip to "enabled" from a peer worker left this node unable to run 

1450 # plugins until restart. Keep initialisation unconditional; gate 

1451 # *execution* on the shared toggle in ``get_plugin_manager``. 

1452 # First-Party 

1453 from mcpgateway.plugins.policy import HOOK_PAYLOAD_POLICIES # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

1454 

1455 try: 

1456 init_plugin_manager_factory( 

1457 yaml_path=settings.plugins.config_file, 

1458 timeout=settings.plugins.plugin_timeout, 

1459 hook_policies=HOOK_PAYLOAD_POLICIES, 

1460 observability=None, # Will be set later if needed 

1461 db_factory=SessionLocal, 

1462 ) 

1463 logger.info("Plugin manager factory initialized") 

1464 except Exception as init_exc: 

1465 if settings.plugins.enabled: 

1466 # Operator asked for plugins — a failed init (bad YAML, missing 

1467 # plugin module, validation error) must be a hard boot failure 

1468 # rather than a silent no-op. Preserve the original loud-crash 

1469 # semantics; the outer lifespan handler logs and re-raises. 

1470 logger.error("Plugin manager factory initialization failed: %s", init_exc, exc_info=True) 

1471 raise 

1472 # Plugins disabled locally; we init opportunistically so a later 

1473 # shared-toggle flip from a peer worker can turn the subsystem on 

1474 # without restarting this node. If that opportunistic init fails, 

1475 # the gateway still boots — but mark the node degraded so 

1476 # ``get_plugin_manager`` emits an ERROR the first time the shared 

1477 # toggle asks us to serve plugins we can't actually run. 

1478 logger.warning( 

1479 "Plugin manager factory init failed (%s); runtime-enable from a peer worker will require this node to restart", 

1480 init_exc, 

1481 ) 

1482 # First-Party 

1483 from mcpgateway.plugins import mark_factory_init_degraded # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

1484 

1485 mark_factory_init_degraded() 

1486 

1487 # Load SpanAttributeCustomizer baggage emission policy before telemetry starts 

1488 # creating spans. Baggage remains the propagation mechanism; this policy 

1489 # controls the span attribute names exported from allowed baggage keys. 

1490 configure_baggage_span_attribute_policy(extract_baggage_span_attribute_policy(get_plugin_manager_factory())) 

1491 init_telemetry() 

1492 logger.info("Observability initialized") 

1493 

1494 try: 

1495 plugin_manager = await get_plugin_manager() 

1496 if plugin_manager: 

1497 logger.info(f"Plugin manager initialized with {plugin_manager.plugin_count} plugins") 

1498 # Wire plugin manager to plugin service for admin endpoints 

1499 # First-Party 

1500 from mcpgateway.services.plugin_service import get_plugin_service # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

1501 

1502 plugin_service = get_plugin_service() 

1503 plugin_service.set_plugin_manager(plugin_manager) 

1504 # Expose on app.state so the admin UI can show the correct enabled status 

1505 app.state.plugin_manager = plugin_manager 

1506 except Exception as diag_exc: 

1507 logger.error(f"Plugin manager initialization failed: {diag_exc}", exc_info=True) 

1508 raise 

1509 

1510 # Always start the invalidation listener when a factory is live, even 

1511 # if the local ``plugins.enabled`` setting is false. The listener 

1512 # early-exits when no Redis provider is registered, so single-node 

1513 # deployments don't spin. 

1514 await start_plugin_invalidation_listener() 

1515 logger.info("Plugin invalidation listener started") 

1516 

1517 # Wire observability adapter to plugin manager if observability is enabled 

1518 if settings.observability_enabled and _service is not None: # pylint: disable=possibly-used-before-assignment 

1519 # First-Party 

1520 from mcpgateway.plugins import set_global_observability # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

1521 from mcpgateway.plugins.observability_adapter import ObservabilityServiceAdapter # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

1522 

1523 set_global_observability(ObservabilityServiceAdapter(service=_service)) 

1524 logger.info("🔍 Plugin observability adapter wired to ObservabilityService") 

1525 

1526 if settings.enable_header_passthrough: 

1527 await setup_passthrough_headers() 

1528 else: 

1529 logger.info("🔒 Header Passthrough: DISABLED") 

1530 

1531 await tool_service.initialize() 

1532 await resource_service.initialize() 

1533 await prompt_service.initialize() 

1534 await gateway_service.initialize() 

1535 

1536 # Start heartbeat, RPC listener, and notification service for 

1537 # multi-worker session affinity. The upstream-session pool is 

1538 # owned by ``UpstreamSessionRegistry`` and runs unconditionally; 

1539 # only the cross-worker affinity machinery is gated here. 

1540 if settings.mcpgateway_session_affinity_enabled: 

1541 # First-Party 

1542 from mcpgateway.services.session_affinity import get_session_affinity, start_affinity_notification_service # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

1543 

1544 await start_affinity_notification_service(gateway_service) 

1545 pool = get_session_affinity() 

1546 pool.start_heartbeat() 

1547 pool._rpc_listener_task = asyncio.create_task(pool.start_rpc_listener()) # pylint: disable=protected-access 

1548 logger.info("Multi-worker session affinity heartbeat and RPC listener started") 

1549 

1550 await root_service.initialize() 

1551 await completion_service.initialize() 

1552 await sampling_handler.initialize() 

1553 await export_service.initialize() 

1554 await import_service.initialize() 

1555 if a2a_service: 

1556 await a2a_service.initialize() 

1557 await resource_cache.initialize() 

1558 await streamable_http_session.initialize() 

1559 await session_registry.initialize() 

1560 

1561 # Initialize OrchestrationService for tool cancellation if enabled 

1562 if settings.mcpgateway_tool_cancellation_enabled: 

1563 await cancellation_service.initialize() 

1564 logger.info("Tool cancellation feature enabled") 

1565 else: 

1566 logger.info("Tool cancellation feature disabled") 

1567 

1568 # Initialize elicitation service 

1569 if settings.mcpgateway_elicitation_enabled: 

1570 # First-Party 

1571 from mcpgateway.services.elicitation_service import get_elicitation_service # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

1572 

1573 elicitation_service = get_elicitation_service() 

1574 await elicitation_service.start() 

1575 logger.info("Elicitation service initialized") 

1576 

1577 # Initialize metrics buffer service for batching metric writes 

1578 if settings.metrics_buffer_enabled: 

1579 # First-Party 

1580 from mcpgateway.services.metrics_buffer_service import get_metrics_buffer_service # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

1581 

1582 metrics_buffer_service = get_metrics_buffer_service() 

1583 await metrics_buffer_service.start() 

1584 if settings.db_metrics_recording_enabled: 

1585 logger.info("Metrics buffer service initialized") 

1586 else: 

1587 logger.info("Metrics buffer service initialized (recording disabled)") 

1588 

1589 # Initialize metrics cleanup service for automatic deletion of old metrics 

1590 if settings.metrics_cleanup_enabled: 

1591 # First-Party 

1592 from mcpgateway.services.metrics_cleanup_service import get_metrics_cleanup_service # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

1593 

1594 metrics_cleanup_service = get_metrics_cleanup_service() 

1595 await metrics_cleanup_service.start() 

1596 logger.info("Metrics cleanup service initialized (retention: %d days)", settings.metrics_retention_days) 

1597 

1598 # Initialize metrics rollup service for hourly aggregation 

1599 if settings.metrics_rollup_enabled: 

1600 # First-Party 

1601 from mcpgateway.services.metrics_rollup_service import get_metrics_rollup_service # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

1602 

1603 metrics_rollup_service = get_metrics_rollup_service() 

1604 await metrics_rollup_service.start() 

1605 logger.info("Metrics rollup service initialized (interval: %dh)", settings.metrics_rollup_interval_hours) 

1606 

1607 refresh_slugs_on_startup() 

1608 

1609 # Bootstrap SSO providers from environment configuration 

1610 if settings.sso_enabled: 

1611 await attempt_to_bootstrap_sso_providers() 

1612 

1613 logger.info("All services initialized successfully") 

1614 

1615 # Warn about unsafe UAID configuration if A2A is enabled 

1616 if settings.mcpgateway_a2a_enabled: 

1617 uaid_allowed_domains = getattr(settings, "uaid_allowed_domains", []) 

1618 if not uaid_allowed_domains: 

1619 logger.warning( 

1620 "⚠️ SECURITY: UAID_ALLOWED_DOMAINS is empty - cross-gateway routing is unrestricted. " 

1621 "This allows UAID-based routing to ANY domain, including internal networks. " 

1622 "Production deployments MUST configure UAID_ALLOWED_DOMAINS to restrict routing to trusted domains only. " 

1623 'Example: UAID_ALLOWED_DOMAINS=["trusted.example.com","gateway.example.org"]' 

1624 ) 

1625 

1626 _install_sighup_handler() 

1627 

1628 # Start cache invalidation subscriber for cross-worker cache synchronization 

1629 # First-Party 

1630 from mcpgateway.cache.registry_cache import get_cache_invalidation_subscriber # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

1631 

1632 cache_invalidation_subscriber = get_cache_invalidation_subscriber() 

1633 await cache_invalidation_subscriber.start() 

1634 

1635 # Start runtime-mode coordinator for cluster-wide override propagation 

1636 # First-Party 

1637 from mcpgateway.runtime_state import get_runtime_state_coordinator # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

1638 

1639 runtime_state_coordinator = get_runtime_state_coordinator() 

1640 await runtime_state_coordinator.start() 

1641 

1642 # Reconfigure uvicorn loggers after startup to capture access logs in dual output 

1643 logging_service.configure_uvicorn_after_startup() 

1644 

1645 if settings.metrics_aggregation_enabled and settings.metrics_aggregation_auto_start: 

1646 aggregation_stop_event = asyncio.Event() 

1647 log_aggregator = get_log_aggregator() 

1648 

1649 async def run_log_backfill() -> None: 

1650 """Backfill log aggregation metrics for configured hours.""" 

1651 hours = getattr(settings, "metrics_aggregation_backfill_hours", 0) 

1652 if hours <= 0: 

1653 return 

1654 try: 

1655 await asyncio.to_thread(log_aggregator.backfill, hours) 

1656 logger.info("Log aggregation backfill completed for last %s hour(s)", hours) 

1657 except Exception as backfill_error: # pragma: no cover - defensive logging 

1658 logger.warning("Log aggregation backfill failed: %s", backfill_error) 

1659 

1660 async def run_log_aggregation_loop() -> None: 

1661 """Run continuous log aggregation at configured intervals. 

1662 

1663 Raises: 

1664 asyncio.CancelledError: When aggregation is stopped 

1665 """ 

1666 interval_seconds = max(1, int(settings.metrics_aggregation_window_minutes)) * 60 

1667 logger.info( 

1668 "Starting log aggregation loop (window=%s min)", 

1669 log_aggregator.aggregation_window_minutes, 

1670 ) 

1671 try: 

1672 while not aggregation_stop_event.is_set(): 

1673 try: 

1674 await asyncio.to_thread(log_aggregator.aggregate_all_components) 

1675 except Exception as agg_error: # pragma: no cover - defensive logging 

1676 logger.warning("Log aggregation loop iteration failed: %s", agg_error) 

1677 

1678 try: 

1679 await asyncio.wait_for(aggregation_stop_event.wait(), timeout=interval_seconds) 

1680 except asyncio.TimeoutError: 

1681 continue 

1682 except asyncio.CancelledError: 

1683 logger.debug("Log aggregation loop cancelled") 

1684 raise 

1685 finally: 

1686 logger.info("Log aggregation loop stopped") 

1687 

1688 aggregation_backfill_task = asyncio.create_task(run_log_backfill()) 

1689 aggregation_loop_task = asyncio.create_task(run_log_aggregation_loop()) 

1690 elif settings.metrics_aggregation_enabled: 

1691 logger.info("Metrics aggregation auto-start disabled; performance metrics will be generated on-demand when requested.") 

1692 

1693 yield 

1694 except Exception as e: 

1695 logger.error(f"Error during startup: {str(e)}") 

1696 # For plugin errors, exit cleanly without stack trace spam 

1697 if "Plugin initialization failed" in str(e): 

1698 # Suppress uvicorn error logging for clean exit 

1699 logging.getLogger("uvicorn.error").setLevel(logging.CRITICAL) 

1700 raise SystemExit(1) 

1701 raise 

1702 finally: 

1703 # Restore default SIGHUP handling in case we reset signal handlers. 

1704 try: 

1705 _restore_default_sighup_handler() 

1706 except Exception as exc: # pragma: no cover - defensive 

1707 logger.debug(f"Failed to restore default SIGHUP handler: {exc}") 

1708 

1709 if aggregation_stop_event is not None: 

1710 aggregation_stop_event.set() 

1711 for task in (aggregation_backfill_task, aggregation_loop_task): 

1712 if task: 

1713 task.cancel() 

1714 with suppress(asyncio.CancelledError): 

1715 await task 

1716 

1717 # Stop the plugin invalidation listener before the factory so in-flight 

1718 # messages don't race with a half-torn-down cache. 

1719 try: 

1720 await stop_plugin_invalidation_listener() 

1721 except Exception as e: 

1722 logger.debug(f"Error stopping plugin invalidation listener: {e}") 

1723 

1724 # Shutdown global plugin manager factory (no-op when plugins were never initialised) 

1725 try: 

1726 await shutdown_plugin_manager_factory() 

1727 logger.info("Plugin manager shutdown complete") 

1728 except Exception as e: 

1729 logger.error(f"Error shutting down plugin manager: {str(e)}") 

1730 

1731 # Stop cache invalidation subscriber 

1732 try: 

1733 # First-Party 

1734 from mcpgateway.cache.registry_cache import get_cache_invalidation_subscriber # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

1735 

1736 cache_invalidation_subscriber = get_cache_invalidation_subscriber() 

1737 await cache_invalidation_subscriber.stop() 

1738 except Exception as e: 

1739 logger.debug(f"Error stopping cache invalidation subscriber: {e}") 

1740 

1741 # Stop runtime-mode coordinator 

1742 try: 

1743 # First-Party 

1744 from mcpgateway.runtime_state import get_runtime_state_coordinator # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

1745 

1746 await get_runtime_state_coordinator().stop() 

1747 except Exception as e: 

1748 logger.debug(f"Error stopping runtime-mode coordinator: {e}") 

1749 

1750 logger.info("Shutting down ContextForge services") 

1751 # await stop_streamablehttp() 

1752 # Build service list conditionally 

1753 services_to_shutdown: List[Any] = [ 

1754 resource_cache, 

1755 sampling_handler, 

1756 import_service, 

1757 export_service, 

1758 logging_service, 

1759 completion_service, 

1760 root_service, 

1761 gateway_service, 

1762 prompt_service, 

1763 resource_service, 

1764 tool_service, 

1765 streamable_http_session, 

1766 session_registry, 

1767 ] 

1768 

1769 if siem_export_service is not None: 

1770 services_to_shutdown.insert(0, siem_export_service) 

1771 

1772 # Add cancellation service if enabled 

1773 if settings.mcpgateway_tool_cancellation_enabled: 

1774 services_to_shutdown.insert(0, cancellation_service) # Shutdown early to stop accepting new cancellations 

1775 

1776 if a2a_service: 

1777 services_to_shutdown.insert(4, a2a_service) # Insert after export_service 

1778 

1779 # Add elicitation service if enabled 

1780 if settings.mcpgateway_elicitation_enabled: 

1781 # First-Party 

1782 from mcpgateway.services.elicitation_service import get_elicitation_service # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

1783 

1784 elicitation_service = get_elicitation_service() 

1785 services_to_shutdown.insert(5, elicitation_service) 

1786 

1787 # Add metrics buffer service if enabled (flush remaining metrics before shutdown) 

1788 if settings.metrics_buffer_enabled: 

1789 # First-Party 

1790 from mcpgateway.services.metrics_buffer_service import get_metrics_buffer_service # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

1791 

1792 metrics_buffer_service = get_metrics_buffer_service() 

1793 services_to_shutdown.insert(0, metrics_buffer_service) # Shutdown first to flush metrics 

1794 

1795 # Add metrics rollup service if enabled (shutdown before cleanup) 

1796 if settings.metrics_rollup_enabled: 

1797 # First-Party 

1798 from mcpgateway.services.metrics_rollup_service import get_metrics_rollup_service # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

1799 

1800 metrics_rollup_service = get_metrics_rollup_service() 

1801 services_to_shutdown.insert(1, metrics_rollup_service) 

1802 

1803 # Add metrics cleanup service if enabled 

1804 if settings.metrics_cleanup_enabled: 

1805 # First-Party 

1806 from mcpgateway.services.metrics_cleanup_service import get_metrics_cleanup_service # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

1807 

1808 metrics_cleanup_service = get_metrics_cleanup_service() 

1809 services_to_shutdown.insert(2, metrics_cleanup_service) 

1810 

1811 await shutdown_services(services_to_shutdown) 

1812 

1813 # Shutdown session-affinity service (before shared HTTP client). 

1814 if settings.mcpgateway_session_affinity_enabled: 

1815 # First-Party 

1816 from mcpgateway.services.session_affinity import close_session_affinity # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

1817 

1818 await close_session_affinity() 

1819 

1820 # Drain upstream session registry (#4205): every (downstream_session_id, 

1821 # gateway_id) → upstream ClientSession owned by this worker is closed. 

1822 # First-Party 

1823 from mcpgateway.services.upstream_session_registry import shutdown_upstream_session_registry # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

1824 

1825 await shutdown_upstream_session_registry() 

1826 

1827 # Shutdown shared HTTP client (after services, before Redis) 

1828 await SharedHttpClient.shutdown() 

1829 

1830 # Close Redis client last (after all services that use it) 

1831 await close_redis_client() 

1832 

1833 logger.info("Shutdown complete") 

1834 

1835 

1836async def shutdown_services(services_to_shutdown: list[Any]): 

1837 """ 

1838 Awaits shutdown of services provided in a list 

1839 

1840 Args: 

1841 services_to_shutdown (list[Any]): list of services to shutdown 

1842 """ 

1843 for service in services_to_shutdown: 

1844 try: 

1845 await service.shutdown() 

1846 except Exception as e: 

1847 logger.error(f"Error shutting down {service.__class__.__name__}: {str(e)}") 

1848 

1849 

1850async def setup_passthrough_headers(): 

1851 """ 

1852 Enables configuration and logs active settings as needed for when passthrough headers are enabled. 

1853 """ 

1854 logger.info(f"🔄 Header Passthrough: ENABLED (default headers: {settings.default_passthrough_headers})") 

1855 if settings.enable_overwrite_base_headers: 

1856 logger.warning("⚠️ Base Header Override: ENABLED - Client headers can override gateway headers") 

1857 else: 

1858 logger.info("🔒 Base Header Override: DISABLED - Gateway headers take precedence") 

1859 db_gen = get_db() 

1860 db = next(db_gen) # pylint: disable=stop-iteration-return 

1861 try: 

1862 await set_global_passthrough_headers(db) 

1863 finally: 

1864 db.commit() # End transaction cleanly 

1865 db.close() 

1866 

1867 

1868# Initialize FastAPI app with orjson for 2-3x faster JSON serialization 

1869app = FastAPI( 

1870 title=settings.app_name, 

1871 version=__version__, 

1872 description="ContextForge AI Gateway — an AI gateway, registry, and proxy for MCP, A2A, and REST/gRPC APIs. Exposes a unified control plane with centralized governance, discovery, and observability. Optimizes agent and tool calling, and supports plugins.", 

1873 root_path=settings.app_root_path, 

1874 lifespan=lifespan, 

1875 default_response_class=ORJSONResponse, # Use orjson for high-performance JSON serialization 

1876) 

1877 

1878# Setup metrics instrumentation 

1879setup_metrics(app) 

1880 

1881 

1882def validate_security_configuration(): 

1883 """ 

1884 Validate security configuration on startup. 

1885 This function encapsulates: 

1886 - verifying the configuration, 

1887 - logging the output for warnings, 

1888 - critical issues 

1889 - security recommendations 

1890 

1891 Args: None 

1892 Raises: Passthrough Errors/Exceptions but doesn't raise any of its own. 

1893 """ 

1894 logger.info("🔒 Validating security configuration...") 

1895 try: 

1896 current_settings = get_settings() 

1897 

1898 security_status: settings.SecurityStatus = current_settings.get_security_status() 

1899 security_warnings = security_status["warnings"] 

1900 

1901 log_security_warnings(security_warnings) 

1902 

1903 # Warn about ephemeral storage without strict user-in-DB mode 

1904 if not getattr(current_settings, "require_user_in_db", False): 

1905 

1906 is_ephemeral = ":memory:" in current_settings.database_url or current_settings.database_url == "sqlite:///./mcp.db" 

1907 if is_ephemeral: 

1908 logger.warning("Using potentially ephemeral storage with platform admin bootstrap enabled. Consider using persistent storage or setting REQUIRE_USER_IN_DB=true for production.") 

1909 

1910 # Warn about default JWT issuer/audience in non-development environments 

1911 if current_settings.environment != "development": 

1912 if current_settings.jwt_issuer == "mcpgateway": 

1913 

1914 logger.warning("Using default JWT_ISSUER in %s environment. Set a unique JWT_ISSUER per environment to prevent cross-environment token acceptance.", current_settings.environment) 

1915 if current_settings.jwt_audience == "mcpgateway-api": 

1916 logger.warning("Using default JWT_AUDIENCE in %s environment. Set a unique JWT_AUDIENCE per environment to prevent cross-environment token acceptance.", current_settings.environment) 

1917 

1918 # UAID Cross-Gateway Routing Security Check 

1919 if not current_settings.uaid_allowed_domains: 

1920 if not current_settings.auth_required: 

1921 logger.error( 

1922 "⚠️ INSECURE CONFIGURATION: UAID_ALLOWED_DOMAINS is empty AND AUTH_REQUIRED=false. " 

1923 "Cross-gateway routing is enabled without domain restrictions or authentication. " 

1924 "This allows UAID-based agents to route to ANY remote gateway without validation. " 

1925 "STRONGLY RECOMMENDED: Set UAID_ALLOWED_DOMAINS to restrict routing to trusted domains only." 

1926 ) 

1927 else: 

1928 logger.warning( 

1929 "⚠️ UAID_ALLOWED_DOMAINS is empty - cross-gateway routing allows ALL domains. " 

1930 "Any UAID-based agent can route to any remote gateway endpoint. " 

1931 "RECOMMENDED: Configure UAID_ALLOWED_DOMAINS to restrict routing to trusted gateways only. " 

1932 'Example: UAID_ALLOWED_DOMAINS=["trusted-gateway.example.com", "partner.org"]' 

1933 ) 

1934 

1935 # Audit logging for explicit security overrides in production 

1936 if current_settings.environment == "production" and not current_settings.require_strong_secrets: 

1937 logger.warning("SECURITY AUDIT: REQUIRE_STRONG_SECRETS is explicitly disabled in a production environment. This override is being logged for audit purposes as per US-1 requirements.") 

1938 

1939 log_security_recommendations(security_status) 

1940 except SecurityConfigurationError as e: 

1941 logger.critical(f"FAIL-CLOSED: {e}") 

1942 sys.exit(1) 

1943 

1944 

1945def log_security_warnings(security_warnings: list[str]): 

1946 """Log warnings from list of security warnings provided. 

1947 

1948 Args: 

1949 security_warnings: List of security warning messages. 

1950 """ 

1951 if security_warnings: 

1952 logger.warning("=" * 60) 

1953 logger.warning("🚨 SECURITY WARNINGS DETECTED:") 

1954 logger.warning("=" * 60) 

1955 for warning in security_warnings: 

1956 logger.warning(f" {warning}") 

1957 logger.warning("=" * 60) 

1958 

1959 

1960def log_critical_issues(critical_issues: list[Any]): 

1961 """ 

1962 Log critical based on configuration settings 

1963 If REQUIRE_STRONG_SECRETS set, this will output critical errors and exit the mcpgateway server. 

1964 

1965 Args: 

1966 critical_issues: List 

1967 

1968 Returns: None 

1969 """ 

1970 # Handle critical issues based on REQUIRE_STRONG_SECRETS setting 

1971 if critical_issues: 

1972 if settings.require_strong_secrets: 

1973 logger.error("=" * 60) 

1974 logger.error("💀 CRITICAL SECURITY ISSUES DETECTED:") 

1975 logger.error("=" * 60) 

1976 for issue in critical_issues: 

1977 logger.error(f" ❌ {issue}") 

1978 logger.error("=" * 60) 

1979 logger.error("Startup aborted due to REQUIRE_STRONG_SECRETS=true") 

1980 logger.error("To proceed anyway, set REQUIRE_STRONG_SECRETS=false") 

1981 logger.error("=" * 60) 

1982 sys.exit(1) 

1983 else: 

1984 # Log as warnings if not enforcing 

1985 logger.warning("=" * 60) 

1986 logger.warning("⚠️ Critical security issues detected (REQUIRE_STRONG_SECRETS=false):") 

1987 for issue in critical_issues: 

1988 logger.warning(f" • {issue}") 

1989 logger.warning("=" * 60) 

1990 

1991 

1992def log_security_recommendations(security_status: settings.SecurityStatus): 

1993 """ 

1994 Log security recommendations based on configuration settings 

1995 

1996 Args: 

1997 security_status (settings.SecurityStatus): The SecurityStatus object for checking and logging current security settings from MCPGateway. 

1998 

1999 Returns: None 

2000 """ 

2001 if not security_status["secure_secrets"] or not security_status["auth_enabled"]: 

2002 logger.info("=" * 60) 

2003 logger.info("📋 SECURITY RECOMMENDATIONS:") 

2004 logger.info("=" * 60) 

2005 

2006 if settings.jwt_secret_key in ("my-test-key", "my-test-key-but-now-longer-than-32-bytes"): # nosec B105 - checking for default value 

2007 logger.info(" • Generate a strong JWT secret:") 

2008 logger.info(" python3 -c 'import secrets; print(secrets.token_urlsafe(32))'") 

2009 

2010 if settings.basic_auth_password.get_secret_value() == "changeme": # nosec B105 - checking for default value 

2011 logger.info(" • Set a strong admin password in BASIC_AUTH_PASSWORD") 

2012 

2013 if not settings.auth_required: 

2014 logger.info(" • Enable authentication: AUTH_REQUIRED=true") 

2015 

2016 if settings.skip_ssl_verify: 

2017 logger.info(" • Enable SSL verification: SKIP_SSL_VERIFY=false") 

2018 

2019 logger.info("=" * 60) 

2020 

2021 

2022def validate_uaid_security_config() -> None: 

2023 """Validate UAID security configuration at startup. 

2024 

2025 Behavior: 

2026 - Logs ERROR if A2A enabled but UAID allowlist not configured 

2027 - Fails startup if UAID_REQUIRE_ALLOWLIST_ON_STARTUP=true (strict mode) 

2028 

2029 Design Decision (Issue #4236, Task #5): 

2030 Default behavior is ERROR logging (non-blocking) to maintain backward compatibility 

2031 and avoid breaking existing deployments. Operators can opt into fail-fast behavior 

2032 via UAID_REQUIRE_ALLOWLIST_ON_STARTUP=true for stricter security posture. 

2033 

2034 Rationale: 

2035 - ERROR logging: Visible in logs, doesn't break deployments 

2036 - Fail-fast (opt-in): Best for production, catches misconfig early 

2037 - Not implemented: Admin UI banner (requires UI work, not always enabled) 

2038 

2039 Raises: 

2040 RuntimeError: If allowlist misconfigured and strict mode enabled 

2041 """ 

2042 if settings.mcpgateway_a2a_enabled: 

2043 if not settings.uaid_allowed_domains and not settings.uaid_allow_all_domains: 

2044 error_msg = ( 

2045 "🚨 SECURITY: UAID cross-gateway routing is DISABLED. " 

2046 "Configure UAID_ALLOWED_DOMAINS with trusted domains or set UAID_ALLOW_ALL_DOMAINS=true (unsafe for production). " 

2047 "Cross-gateway UAID calls will fail until allowlist is configured." 

2048 ) 

2049 

2050 logger.error(error_msg) 

2051 

2052 # Check for strict mode (fail-fast on misconfiguration) 

2053 if settings.uaid_require_allowlist_on_startup: 

2054 raise RuntimeError( 

2055 f"{error_msg}\n\n" 

2056 "Gateway startup aborted due to UAID_REQUIRE_ALLOWLIST_ON_STARTUP=true. " 

2057 "Fix configuration or set UAID_REQUIRE_ALLOWLIST_ON_STARTUP=false to allow startup with ERROR log only." 

2058 ) 

2059 

2060 logger.info("✅ Security validation completed") 

2061 

2062 

2063# Global exceptions handlers 

2064@app.exception_handler(ValidationError)
