11577 tag_name: str, 

11578 entity_types: Optional[str] = None, 

11579 db: Session = Depends(get_db), 

11580 user=Depends(get_current_user_with_permissions), 

11581) -> List[TaggedEntity]: 

11582 """ 

11583 Get all entities that have a specific tag. 

11584 

11585 Args: 

11586 request: FastAPI request object used to derive token/team visibility scope 

11587 tag_name: The tag to search for 

11588 entity_types: Comma-separated list of entity types to filter by 

11589 (e.g., "tools,resources,prompts,servers,gateways"). 

11590 If not provided, returns entities from all types. 

11591 db: Database session 

11592 user: Authenticated user 

11593 

11594 Returns: 

11595 List of TaggedEntity objects 

11596 

11597 Raises: 

11598 HTTPException: If entity retrieval fails 

11599 """ 

11600 # Parse entity types parameter if provided 

11601 entity_types_list = None 

11602 if entity_types: 

11603 entity_types_list = [et.strip().lower() for et in entity_types.split(",") if et.strip()] 

11604 

11605 logger.debug(f"User {SecurityValidator.sanitize_log_message(str(user))} is retrieving entities for tag '{tag_name}' with entity types: {entity_types_list}") 

11606 

11607 try: 

11608 user_email, token_teams, is_admin = get_rpc_filter_context(request, user) 

11609 if is_admin and token_teams is None: 

11610 user_email = None 

11611 elif token_teams is None: 

11612 token_teams = [] 

11613 

11614 entities = await tag_service.get_entities_by_tag( 

11615 db, 

11616 tag_name=tag_name, 

11617 entity_types=entity_types_list, 

11618 user_email=user_email, 

11619 token_teams=token_teams, 

11620 ) 

11621 return entities 

11622 except Exception as e: 

11623 logger.error(f"Failed to retrieve entities for tag '{tag_name}': {str(e)}") 

11624 raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail="Failed to retrieve entities") 

11625 

11626 

11627#################### 

11628# Export/Import # 

11629#################### 

11630 

11631 

11632@export_import_router.get("/export", response_model=Dict[str, Any]) 

11633@require_permission("admin.export") 

11634async def export_configuration( 

11635 request: Request, # pylint: disable=unused-argument 

11636 export_format: str = "json", # pylint: disable=unused-argument 

11637 types: Optional[str] = None, 

11638 exclude_types: Optional[str] = None, 

11639 tags: Optional[str] = None, 

11640 include_inactive: bool = False, 

11641 include_dependencies: bool = True, 

11642 db: Session = Depends(get_db), 

11643 user=Depends(get_current_user_with_permissions), 

11644) -> Dict[str, Any]: 

11645 """ 

11646 Export gateway configuration to JSON format. 

11647 

11648 Args: 

11649 request: FastAPI request object for extracting root path 

11650 export_format: Export format (currently only 'json' supported) 

11651 types: Comma-separated list of entity types to include (tools,gateways,servers,prompts,resources,roots) 

11652 exclude_types: Comma-separated list of entity types to exclude 

11653 tags: Comma-separated list of tags to filter by 

11654 include_inactive: Whether to include inactive entities 

11655 include_dependencies: Whether to include dependent entities 

11656 db: Database session 

11657 user: Authenticated user 

11658 

11659 Returns: 

11660 Export data in the specified format 

11661 

11662 Raises: 

11663 HTTPException: If export fails 

11664 """ 

11665 try: 

11666 logger.info(f"User {SecurityValidator.sanitize_log_message(str(user))} requested configuration export") 

11667 username: Optional[str] = None 

11668 # Parse parameters 

11669 include_types = None 

11670 if types: 

11671 include_types = [t.strip() for t in types.split(",") if t.strip()] 

11672 

11673 exclude_types_list = None 

11674 if exclude_types: 

11675 exclude_types_list = [t.strip() for t in exclude_types.split(",") if t.strip()] 

11676 

11677 tags_list = None 

11678 if tags: 

11679 tags_list = [t.strip() for t in tags.split(",") if t.strip()] 

11680 

11681 # Extract username from user (which is now an EmailUser object) 

11682 if hasattr(user, "email"): 

11683 username = getattr(user, "email", None) 

11684 elif isinstance(user, dict): 

11685 username = user.get("email", None) 

11686 else: 

11687 username = None 

11688 

11689 # Get root path for URL construction - prefer configured APP_ROOT_PATH 

11690 root_path = settings.app_root_path 

11691 

11692 # Derive team-scoped visibility from the requesting user's token 

11693 scoped_user_email, scoped_token_teams = get_scoped_resource_access_context(request, user) 

11694 

11695 # Perform export 

11696 export_data = await export_service.export_configuration( 

11697 db=db, 

11698 include_types=include_types, 

11699 exclude_types=exclude_types_list, 

11700 tags=tags_list, 

11701 include_inactive=include_inactive, 

11702 include_dependencies=include_dependencies, 

11703 exported_by=username or "unknown", 

11704 root_path=root_path, 

11705 user_email=scoped_user_email, 

11706 token_teams=scoped_token_teams, 

11707 ) 

11708 

11709 return export_data 

11710 

11711 except ExportError as e: 

11712 logger.error(f"Export failed for user {SecurityValidator.sanitize_log_message(str(user))}: {str(e)}") 

11713 raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=str(e)) 

11714 except Exception as e: 

11715 logger.error(f"Unexpected export error for user {SecurityValidator.sanitize_log_message(str(user))}: {str(e)}") 

11716 raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail="Export failed") 

11717 

11718 

11719@export_import_router.post("/export/selective", response_model=Dict[str, Any]) 

11720@require_permission("admin.export") 

11721async def export_selective_configuration( 

11722 request: Request, entity_selections: Dict[str, List[str]] = Body(...), include_dependencies: bool = True, db: Session = Depends(get_db), user=Depends(get_current_user_with_permissions) 

11723) -> Dict[str, Any]: 

11724 """ 

11725 Export specific entities by their IDs/names. 

11726 

11727 Args: 

11728 request: FastAPI request object for token scope context 

11729 entity_selections: Dict mapping entity types to lists of IDs/names to export 

11730 include_dependencies: Whether to include dependent entities 

11731 db: Database session 

11732 user: Authenticated user 

11733 

11734 Returns: 

11735 Selective export data 

11736 

11737 Raises: 

11738 HTTPException: If export fails 

11739 

11740 Example request body: 

11741 { 

11742 "tools": ["tool1", "tool2"], 

11743 "servers": ["server1"], 

11744 "prompts": ["prompt1"] 

11745 } 

11746 """ 

11747 try: 

11748 logger.info(f"User {SecurityValidator.sanitize_log_message(str(user))} requested selective configuration export") 

11749 

11750 username: Optional[str] = None 

11751 # Extract username from user (which is now an EmailUser object) 

11752 if hasattr(user, "email"): 

11753 username = getattr(user, "email", None) 

11754 elif isinstance(user, dict): 

11755 username = user.get("email") 

11756 

11757 # Get root path for URL construction - prefer configured APP_ROOT_PATH 

11758 root_path = settings.app_root_path 

11759 

11760 # Derive team-scoped visibility from the requesting user's token 

11761 scoped_user_email, scoped_token_teams = get_scoped_resource_access_context(request, user) 

11762 

11763 export_data = await export_service.export_selective( 

11764 db=db, 

11765 entity_selections=entity_selections, 

11766 include_dependencies=include_dependencies, 

11767 exported_by=username or "unknown", 

11768 root_path=root_path, 

11769 user_email=scoped_user_email, 

11770 token_teams=scoped_token_teams, 

11771 ) 

11772 

11773 return export_data 

11774 

11775 except ExportError as e: 

11776 logger.error(f"Selective export failed for user {SecurityValidator.sanitize_log_message(str(user))}: {str(e)}") 

11777 raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=str(e)) 

11778 except Exception as e: 

11779 logger.error(f"Unexpected selective export error for user {SecurityValidator.sanitize_log_message(str(user))}: {str(e)}") 

11780 raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail="Export failed") 

11781 

11782 

11783@export_import_router.post("/import", response_model=Dict[str, Any]) 

11784@require_permission("admin.import") 

11785async def import_configuration( 

11786 import_data: Dict[str, Any] = Body(...), 

11787 conflict_strategy: str = Body("update"), 

11788 dry_run: bool = Body(False), 

11789 rekey_secret: Optional[str] = Body(None), 

11790 selected_entities: Optional[Dict[str, List[str]]] = Body(None), 

11791 db: Session = Depends(get_db), 

11792 user=Depends(get_current_user_with_permissions), 

11793) -> Dict[str, Any]: 

11794 """ 

11795 Import configuration data with conflict resolution. 

11796 

11797 Args: 

11798 import_data: The configuration data to import 

11799 conflict_strategy: How to handle conflicts: skip, update, rename, fail 

11800 dry_run: If true, validate but don't make changes 

11801 rekey_secret: New encryption secret for cross-environment imports 

11802 selected_entities: Dict of entity types to specific entity names/ids to import 

11803 db: Database session 

11804 user: Authenticated user 

11805 

11806 Returns: 

11807 Import status and results 

11808 

11809 Raises: 

11810 HTTPException: If import fails or validation errors occur 

11811 """ 

11812 try: 

11813 if not import_data: 

11814 raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Missing 'import_data' in request body") 

11815 

11816 logger.info(f"User {SecurityValidator.sanitize_log_message(str(user))} requested configuration import (dry_run={dry_run})") 

11817 

11818 # Validate conflict strategy 

11819 try: 

11820 strategy = ConflictStrategy(conflict_strategy.lower()) 

11821 except ValueError: 

11822 raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=f"Invalid conflict strategy. Must be one of: {[s.value for s in list(ConflictStrategy)]}") 

11823 

11824 # Extract username from user (which is now an EmailUser object) 

11825 if hasattr(user, "email"): 

11826 username = getattr(user, "email", None) 

11827 elif isinstance(user, dict): 

11828 username = user.get("email", None) 

11829 else: 

11830 username = None 

11831 

11832 # Perform import 

11833 import_status = await import_service.import_configuration( 

11834 db=db, import_data=import_data, conflict_strategy=strategy, dry_run=dry_run, rekey_secret=rekey_secret, imported_by=username or "unknown", selected_entities=selected_entities 

11835 ) 

11836 

11837 return import_status.to_dict() 

11838 

11839 except HTTPException: 

11840 raise 

11841 except ImportValidationError as e: 

11842 logger.error(f"Import validation failed for user {SecurityValidator.sanitize_log_message(str(user))}: {str(e)}") 

11843 raise HTTPException(status_code=422, detail="Import validation failed") 

11844 except ImportConflictError as e: 

11845 logger.error(f"Import conflict for user {SecurityValidator.sanitize_log_message(str(user))}: {str(e)}") 

11846 raise HTTPException(status_code=409, detail="Import conflict detected") 

11847 except ImportServiceError as e: 

11848 logger.error(f"Import failed for user {SecurityValidator.sanitize_log_message(str(user))}: {str(e)}") 

11849 raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Import failed") 

11850 except Exception as e: 

11851 logger.error(f"Unexpected import error for user {SecurityValidator.sanitize_log_message(str(user))}: {str(e)}") 

11852 raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail="Import failed") 

11853 

11854 

11855@export_import_router.get("/import/status/{import_id}", response_model=Dict[str, Any]) 

11856@require_permission("admin.import") 

11857async def get_import_status(import_id: str, user=Depends(get_current_user_with_permissions)) -> Dict[str, Any]: 

11858 """ 

11859 Get the status of an import operation. 

11860 

11861 Args: 

11862 import_id: The import operation ID 

11863 user: Authenticated user 

11864 

11865 Returns: 

11866 Import status information 

11867 

11868 Raises: 

11869 HTTPException: If import not found 

11870 """ 

11871 logger.debug(f"User {SecurityValidator.sanitize_log_message(str(user))} requested import status for {import_id}") 

11872 

11873 import_status = import_service.get_import_status(import_id) 

11874 if not import_status: 

11875 raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f"Import {import_id} not found") 

11876 

11877 return import_status.to_dict() 

11878 

11879 

11880@export_import_router.get("/import/status", response_model=List[Dict[str, Any]]) 

11881@require_permission("admin.import") 

11882async def list_import_statuses(user=Depends(get_current_user_with_permissions)) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]: 

11883 """ 

11884 List all import operation statuses. 

11885 

11886 Args: 

11887 user: Authenticated user 

11888 

11889 Returns: 

11890 List of import status information 

11891 """ 

11892 logger.debug(f"User {SecurityValidator.sanitize_log_message(str(user))} requested all import statuses") 

11893 

11894 statuses = import_service.list_import_statuses() 

11895 return [status.to_dict() for status in statuses] 

11896 

11897 

11898@export_import_router.post("/import/cleanup", response_model=Dict[str, Any]) 

11899@require_permission("admin.import") 

11900async def cleanup_import_statuses(max_age_hours: int = 24, user=Depends(get_current_user_with_permissions)) -> Dict[str, Any]: 

11901 """ 

11902 Clean up completed import statuses older than specified age. 

11903 

11904 Args: 

11905 max_age_hours: Maximum age in hours for keeping completed imports 

11906 user: Authenticated user 

11907 

11908 Returns: 

11909 Cleanup results 

11910 """ 

11911 logger.info(f"User {SecurityValidator.sanitize_log_message(str(user))} requested import status cleanup (max_age_hours={max_age_hours})") 

11912 

11913 removed_count = import_service.cleanup_completed_imports(max_age_hours) 

11914 return {"status": "success", "message": f"Cleaned up {removed_count} completed import statuses", "removed_count": removed_count} 

11915 

11916 

11917# Mount static files 

11918# app.mount("/static", StaticFiles(directory=str(settings.static_dir)), name="static") 

11919 

11920# Include routers 

11921app.include_router(version_router) 

11922app.include_router(protocol_router) 

11923app.include_router(tool_router) 

11924app.include_router(resource_router) 

11925app.include_router(prompt_router) 

11926app.include_router(gateway_router) 

11927app.include_router(root_router) 

11928app.include_router(utility_router) 

11929app.include_router(server_router) 

11930app.include_router(server_well_known_router, prefix="/servers") 

11931app.include_router(metrics_router) 

11932app.include_router(tag_router) 

11933app.include_router(export_import_router) 

11934 

11935# Compliance report router (admin API) 

11936if settings.mcpgateway_admin_api_enabled: 

11937 try: 

11938 # First-Party 

11939 from mcpgateway.routers.compliance_router import router as compliance_router 

11940 

11941 app.include_router(compliance_router) 

11942 logger.info("Compliance router included") 

11943 except ImportError as e: # pragma: no cover - optional import guard 

11944 logger.warning(f"Compliance router not available: {e}") 

11945else: 

11946 logger.info("Compliance router not included - admin API disabled") 

11947 

11948# Tool plugin bindings router 

11949try: 

11950 # First-Party 

11951 from mcpgateway.routers.tool_plugin_bindings import router as tool_plugin_bindings_router # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

11952 

11953 app.include_router(tool_plugin_bindings_router) 

11954 logger.info("Tool plugin bindings router included") 

11955except ImportError as e: 

11956 logger.error(f"Tool plugin bindings router not available: {e}") 

11957 

11958# A2A agent plugin bindings router 

11959try: 

11960 # First-Party 

11961 from mcpgateway.routers.a2a_agent_plugin_bindings import router as a2a_agent_plugin_bindings_router # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

11962 

11963 app.include_router(a2a_agent_plugin_bindings_router) 

11964 logger.info("A2A agent plugin bindings router included") 

11965except ImportError as e: 

11966 logger.error(f"A2A agent plugin bindings router not available: {e}") 

11967 

11968# Include log search router if structured logging is enabled 

11969if getattr(settings, "structured_logging_enabled", True): 

11970 try: 

11971 # First-Party 

11972 from mcpgateway.routers.log_search import router as log_search_router 

11973 

11974 app.include_router(log_search_router) 

11975 logger.info("Log search router included - structured logging enabled") 

11976 except ImportError as e: 

11977 logger.warning(f"Failed to import log search router: {e}") 

11978else: 

11979 logger.info("Log search router not included - structured logging disabled") 

11980 

11981# Include SIEM admin router for destination management and health endpoints 

11982if settings.mcpgateway_admin_api_enabled and settings.siem_export_enabled: 

11983 try: 

11984 # First-Party 

11985 from mcpgateway.admin import enforce_admin_csrf # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

11986 from mcpgateway.routers.siem import router as siem_router 

11987 

11988 app.include_router(siem_router, dependencies=[Depends(enforce_admin_csrf)]) 

11989 logger.info("SIEM router included") 

11990 except ImportError as e: # pragma: no cover - optional import guard 

11991 logger.warning(f"SIEM router not available: {e}") 

11992else: 

11993 logger.info("SIEM router not included - admin API or SIEM export disabled") 

11994 

11995# Conditionally include observability router if enabled 

11996if settings.observability_enabled: 

11997 # First-Party 

11998 from mcpgateway.routers.observability import router as observability_router 

11999 

12000 app.include_router(observability_router) 

12001 logger.info("Observability router included - observability API endpoints enabled") 

12002else: 

12003 logger.info("Observability router not included - observability disabled") 

12004 

12005# Conditionally include metrics maintenance router if cleanup or rollup is enabled 

12006if settings.metrics_cleanup_enabled or settings.metrics_rollup_enabled: 

12007 # First-Party 

12008 from mcpgateway.routers.metrics_maintenance import router as metrics_maintenance_router 

12009 

12010 app.include_router(metrics_maintenance_router) 

12011 logger.info("Metrics maintenance router included - cleanup/rollup API endpoints enabled") 

12012 

12013# Conditionally include A2A router if A2A features are enabled 

12014if settings.mcpgateway_a2a_enabled: 

12015 app.include_router(a2a_router) 

12016 logger.info("A2A router included - A2A features enabled") 

12017else: 

12018 logger.info("A2A router not included - A2A features disabled") 

12019 

12020app.include_router(well_known_router) 

12021 

12022# Include Email Authentication router if enabled 

12023if settings.email_auth_enabled: 

12024 try: 

12025 # First-Party 

12026 from mcpgateway.routers.auth import auth_router 

12027 from mcpgateway.routers.email_auth import email_auth_router 

12028 

12029 app.include_router(email_auth_router, prefix="/auth/email", tags=["Email Authentication"]) 

12030 app.include_router(auth_router, tags=["Main Authentication"]) 

12031 logger.info("Authentication routers included - Auth enabled") 

12032 

12033 # Include SSO router if enabled 

12034 if settings.sso_enabled: 

12035 try: 

12036 # First-Party 

12037 from mcpgateway.routers.sso import sso_router 

12038 

12039 app.include_router(sso_router, tags=["SSO Authentication"]) 

12040 logger.info("SSO router included - SSO authentication enabled") 

12041 except ImportError as e: 

12042 logger.error(f"SSO router not available: {e}") 

12043 else: 

12044 logger.info("SSO router not included - SSO authentication disabled") 

12045 except ImportError as e: 

12046 logger.error(f"Authentication routers not available: {e}") 

12047else: 

12048 logger.info("Email authentication router not included - Email auth disabled") 

12049 

12050# Include Team Management router if email auth is enabled 

12051if settings.email_auth_enabled: 

12052 try: 

12053 # First-Party 

12054 from mcpgateway.routers.teams import teams_router 

12055 

12056 app.include_router(teams_router, prefix="/teams", tags=["Teams"]) 

12057 logger.info("Team management router included - Teams enabled with email auth") 

12058 except ImportError as e: 

12059 logger.error(f"Team management router not available: {e}") 

12060else: 

12061 logger.info("Team management router not included - Email auth disabled") 

12062 

12063# Include JWT Token Catalog router if email auth is enabled 

12064if settings.email_auth_enabled: 

12065 try: 

12066 # First-Party 

12067 from mcpgateway.routers.tokens import router as tokens_router 

12068 

12069 app.include_router(tokens_router, tags=["JWT Token Catalog"]) 

12070 logger.info("JWT Token Catalog router included - Token management enabled with email auth") 

12071 except ImportError as e: 

12072 logger.error(f"JWT Token Catalog router not available: {e}") 

12073else: 

12074 logger.info("JWT Token Catalog router not included - Email auth disabled") 

12075 

12076# Include RBAC router if email auth is enabled 

12077if settings.email_auth_enabled: 

12078 try: 

12079 # First-Party 

12080 from mcpgateway.routers.rbac import router as rbac_router 

12081 

12082 app.include_router(rbac_router, tags=["RBAC"]) 

12083 logger.info("RBAC router included - Role-based access control enabled") 

12084 except ImportError as e: 

12085 logger.error(f"RBAC router not available: {e}") 

12086else: 

12087 logger.info("RBAC router not included - Email auth disabled") 

12088 

12089# Include OAuth router 

12090try: 

12091 # First-Party 

12092 from mcpgateway.routers.oauth_router import oauth_router 

12093 

12094 app.include_router(oauth_router) 

12095 logger.info("OAuth router included") 

12096except ImportError: 

12097 logger.debug("OAuth router not available") 

12098 

12099# Include reverse proxy router if enabled 

12100if settings.mcpgateway_reverse_proxy_enabled: 

12101 try: 

12102 # First-Party 

12103 from mcpgateway.routers.reverse_proxy import router as reverse_proxy_router 

12104 

12105 app.include_router(reverse_proxy_router) 

12106 logger.info("Reverse proxy router included") 

12107 except ImportError: 

12108 logger.debug("Reverse proxy router not available") 

12109else: 

12110 logger.info("Reverse proxy router not included - feature disabled") 

12111 

12112# Include LLMChat router 

12113if settings.llmchat_enabled: 

12114 try: 

12115 # First-Party 

12116 from mcpgateway.routers.llmchat_router import llmchat_router 

12117 

12118 app.include_router(llmchat_router) 

12119 logger.info("LLM Chat router included") 

12120 except ImportError: 

12121 logger.debug("LLM Chat router not available") 

12122 

12123 # Include LLM configuration and proxy routers (internal API) 

12124 try: 

12125 # First-Party 

12126 from mcpgateway.admin import enforce_admin_csrf # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

12127 from mcpgateway.routers.llm_admin_router import llm_admin_router 

12128 from mcpgateway.routers.llm_config_router import llm_config_router 

12129 from mcpgateway.routers.llm_proxy_router import llm_proxy_router 

12130 

12131 app.include_router(llm_config_router, prefix="/llm", tags=["LLM Configuration"]) 

12132 app.include_router(llm_proxy_router, prefix=settings.llm_api_prefix, tags=["LLM Proxy"]) 

12133 app.include_router(llm_admin_router, prefix="/admin/llm", tags=["LLM Admin"], dependencies=[Depends(enforce_admin_csrf)]) 

12134 logger.info("LLM configuration, proxy, and admin routers included") 

12135 except ImportError as e: 

12136 logger.debug(f"LLM routers not available: {e}") 

12137 

12138# Include Toolops router 

12139if settings.toolops_enabled: 

12140 try: 

12141 # First-Party 

12142 from mcpgateway.routers.toolops_router import toolops_router 

12143 

12144 app.include_router(toolops_router) 

12145 logger.info("Toolops router included") 

12146 except ImportError: 

12147 logger.debug("Toolops router not available") 

12148 

12149# Cancellation router (tool cancellation endpoints) 

12150if settings.mcpgateway_tool_cancellation_enabled: 

12151 try: 

12152 # First-Party 

12153 from mcpgateway.routers.cancellation_router import router as cancellation_router 

12154 

12155 app.include_router(cancellation_router) 

12156 logger.info("Cancellation router included (tool cancellation enabled)") 

12157 except ImportError: 

12158 logger.debug("Orchestrate router not available") 

12159else: 

12160 logger.info("Tool cancellation feature disabled - cancellation endpoints not available") 

12161 

12162# Feature flags for admin UI and API 

12163UI_ENABLED = settings.mcpgateway_ui_enabled 

12164ADMIN_API_ENABLED = settings.mcpgateway_admin_api_enabled 

12165logger.info(f"Admin UI enabled: {UI_ENABLED}") 

12166logger.info(f"Admin API enabled: {ADMIN_API_ENABLED}") 

12167 

12168# Conditional UI and admin API handling 

12169if ADMIN_API_ENABLED: 

12170 logger.info("Including admin_router - Admin API enabled") 

12171 # Lazy import: mcpgateway.admin is a large module (~19k lines, ~120ms cold). 

12172 # Only load it when the admin API is actually mounted. 

12173 # First-Party 

12174 from mcpgateway.admin import admin_router, enforce_admin_csrf, set_logging_service, validate_section_permissions # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

12175 

12176 set_logging_service(logging_service) 

12177 app.include_router(admin_router) # Admin routes imported from admin.py 

12178 

12179 # Validate section-to-permission mapping consistency at startup 

12180 validate_section_permissions(admin_router) 

12181 

12182 # Runtime-mode admin endpoints (GET/PATCH /admin/runtime/{mcp,a2a}-mode). 

12183 # First-Party 

12184 from mcpgateway.routers.runtime_admin_router import runtime_admin_router # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

12185 

12186 app.include_router(runtime_admin_router, prefix="/admin/runtime", tags=["Runtime Admin"], dependencies=[Depends(enforce_admin_csrf)]) 

12187else: 

12188 logger.warning("Admin API routes not mounted - Admin API disabled via MCPGATEWAY_ADMIN_API_ENABLED=False") 

12189 

12190 

12191class MCPRuntimeHeaderTransportWrapper: 

12192 """Annotate Python-owned MCP transport responses with the active runtime marker.""" 

12193 

12194 def __init__(self, transport_app, *, runtime_name: str) -> None: 

12195 """Wrap an MCP transport app and stamp a runtime header on responses. 

12196 

12197 Args: 

12198 transport_app: Underlying MCP transport app. 

12199 runtime_name: Runtime label to expose via response headers. 

12200 """ 

12201 self.transport_app = transport_app 

12202 self.runtime_name = runtime_name.encode("ascii") 

12203 

12204 async def handle_streamable_http(self, scope, receive, send): 

12205 """Forward an MCP request while ensuring the runtime marker header is present. 

12206 

12207 Args: 

12208 scope: Incoming ASGI scope. 

12209 receive: ASGI receive callable. 

12210 send: ASGI send callable. 

12211 """ 

12212 

12213 async def _send_with_runtime_header(message): 

12214 """Attach MCP runtime mode headers before sending the ASGI event downstream. 

12215 

12216 Args: 

12217 message: Outgoing ASGI message emitted by the wrapped application. 

12218 """ 

12219 if message.get("type") == "http.response.start": 

12220 headers = list(message.get("headers") or []) 

12221 if not any(isinstance(item, (tuple, list)) and len(item) == 2 and isinstance(item[0], (bytes, bytearray)) and item[0].lower() == b"x-contextforge-mcp-runtime" for item in headers): 

12222 headers.append((b"x-contextforge-mcp-runtime", self.runtime_name)) 

12223 if not any( 

12224 isinstance(item, (tuple, list)) and len(item) == 2 and isinstance(item[0], (bytes, bytearray)) and item[0].lower() == b"x-contextforge-mcp-session-core" for item in headers 

12225 ): 

12226 headers.append((b"x-contextforge-mcp-session-core", _current_mcp_session_core_mode().encode("ascii"))) 

12227 if not any(isinstance(item, (tuple, list)) and len(item) == 2 and isinstance(item[0], (bytes, bytearray)) and item[0].lower() == b"x-contextforge-mcp-resume-core" for item in headers): 

12228 headers.append((b"x-contextforge-mcp-resume-core", _current_mcp_resume_core_mode().encode("ascii"))) 

12229 if not any( 

12230 isinstance(item, (tuple, list)) and len(item) == 2 and isinstance(item[0], (bytes, bytearray)) and item[0].lower() == b"x-contextforge-mcp-live-stream-core" for item in headers 

12231 ): 

12232 headers.append((b"x-contextforge-mcp-live-stream-core", _current_mcp_live_stream_core_mode().encode("ascii"))) 

12233 if not any( 

12234 isinstance(item, (tuple, list)) and len(item) == 2 and isinstance(item[0], (bytes, bytearray)) and item[0].lower() == b"x-contextforge-mcp-affinity-core" for item in headers 

12235 ): 

12236 headers.append((b"x-contextforge-mcp-affinity-core", _current_mcp_affinity_core_mode().encode("ascii"))) 

12237 if not any( 

12238 isinstance(item, (tuple, list)) and len(item) == 2 and isinstance(item[0], (bytes, bytearray)) and item[0].lower() == b"x-contextforge-mcp-session-auth-reuse" for item in headers 

12239 ): 

12240 headers.append((b"x-contextforge-mcp-session-auth-reuse", _current_mcp_session_auth_reuse_mode().encode("ascii"))) 

12241 message = dict(message) 

12242 message["headers"] = headers 

12243 await send(message) 

12244 

12245 await self.transport_app.handle_streamable_http(scope, receive, _send_with_runtime_header) 

12246 

12247 

12248def _select_mcp_ingress(_scope: dict) -> str: 

12249 """Pick the registered MCPIngressMount ingress to serve a request. 

12250 

12251 Single source of truth for the dispatch policy: 

12252 

12253 - Boot ``off`` / ``full`` (no dispatcher today): the mount isn't used; 

12254 the Python transport or the plain Rust proxy is mounted directly 

12255 from ``_build_mcp_transport_app``. 

12256 - Boot ``shadow`` / ``edge`` with no override OR an ``edge`` override 

12257 that satisfies the safety invariant: route to the Rust ingress 

12258 shape selected by ``settings.mcp_rust_ingress`` (``"public"`` for 

12259 nginx-style or ``"internal"`` for trusted Python→Rust forwarding). 

12260 - Override forces ``shadow``, OR safety invariant is unmet: route to 

12261 the Python transport (the always-safe fallback). 

12262 

12263 The ``"rust-public"`` ingress is only registered on ``boot=edge`` 

12264 (the public listener isn't bound on shadow boot per the entrypoint 

12265 flow). On any other boot mode the selector transparently downgrades 

12266 a configured ``"public"`` choice to ``"rust-internal"`` to avoid 

12267 routing to an unregistered name; the misconfig itself is surfaced 

12268 as a boot-time error in ``_build_mcp_transport_app``. 

12269 

12270 Args: 

12271 _scope: ASGI scope (unused today; reserved so future selectors 

12272 can route by method/path/headers without changing the 

12273 mount's API). 

12274 

12275 Returns: 

12276 The ingress name to look up in the mount's registry. 

12277 """ 

12278 if not _should_mount_public_rust_transport(): 

12279 return "python" 

12280 if settings.mcp_rust_ingress == "public" and version_module.boot_mcp_runtime_mode() == "edge": 

12281 return "rust-public" 

12282 return "rust-internal" 

12283 

12284 

12285def _build_mcp_transport_app(): 

12286 """Build the ASGI app to mount at public ``/mcp``. 

12287 

12288 Returns: 

12289 For boot modes ``shadow``/``edge``: an :class:`MCPIngressMount` 

12290 with the Python transport, the trusted-internal Rust proxy, and 

12291 (when supported) the nginx-style Rust public proxy registered. 

12292 For boot ``full``: the plain trusted-internal Rust proxy mounted 

12293 directly (no dispatcher — flipping ``full`` would orphan 

12294 Rust-held session/event-store state). For boot ``off``: the 

12295 Python transport. The ``/mcp`` mount calls 

12296 ``returned_app.handle_streamable_http`` (legacy interface kept 

12297 for backward compatibility with the existing mount line). 

12298 """ 

12299 # First-Party 

12300 from mcpgateway.transports.mcp_ingress_mount import MCPIngressMount # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

12301 

12302 boot_mode = version_module.boot_mcp_runtime_mode() 

12303 python_transport = MCPRuntimeHeaderTransportWrapper(streamable_http_session, runtime_name="python") 

12304 

12305 if boot_mode in ("shadow", "edge"): 

12306 # First-Party 

12307 from mcpgateway.transports.rust_mcp_runtime_proxy import RustMCPRuntimeProxy # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

12308 

12309 rust_internal = RustMCPRuntimeProxy(streamable_http_session.handle_streamable_http) 

12310 ingress = MCPIngressMount(selector=_select_mcp_ingress, fallback=python_transport.handle_streamable_http) 

12311 ingress.register("python", python_transport.handle_streamable_http) 

12312 ingress.register("rust-internal", rust_internal.handle_streamable_http) 

12313 

12314 # Public-listener proxy is only meaningful when the safety invariant 

12315 # is met (i.e. boot=edge); shadow boot doesn't bind the public 

12316 # listener. Register it on edge so an operator can flip 

12317 # `settings.mcp_rust_ingress = "public"` without a restart. 

12318 if boot_mode == "edge": 

12319 # First-Party 

12320 from mcpgateway.transports.rust_mcp_public_proxy import build_rust_public_proxy_app # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

12321 

12322 ingress.register("rust-public", build_rust_public_proxy_app()) 

12323 elif settings.mcp_rust_ingress == "public": 

12324 # boot=shadow with mcp_rust_ingress=public is a misconfig: the 

12325 # Rust public listener isn't bound on shadow boot, so the 

12326 # selector deliberately downgrades to "rust-internal". Logged 

12327 # at error severity so it survives the default LOG_LEVEL=ERROR 

12328 # — a warning here would be invisible in most production 

12329 # deployments and the operator would never know their setting 

12330 # is being silently overridden. 

12331 logger.error( 

12332 "mcp_rust_ingress=public is set on boot=shadow; the Rust public listener isn't bound on shadow boot. " 

12333 "Selector will route to rust-internal instead. Switch boot mode to edge to honor the public ingress.", 

12334 ) 

12335 

12336 logger.warning( 

12337 "MCP runtime mode: %s (boot=%s). Public /mcp dispatches via MCPIngressMount; ingresses=%s; current=%s. Runtime override may flip via PATCH /admin/runtime/mcp-mode.", 

12338 _current_mcp_runtime_mode(), 

12339 boot_mode, 

12340 ingress.names(), 

12341 _select_mcp_ingress({}), 

12342 ) 

12343 # The legacy mount line calls .handle_streamable_http on the returned 

12344 # app; expose that name on a tiny shim so the mount line can stay 

12345 # unchanged. (.dispatch is the modern ASGI 3.0 callable.) 

12346 ingress.handle_streamable_http = ingress.dispatch # type: ignore[attr-defined] 

12347 return ingress 

12348 

12349 if _should_mount_public_rust_transport(): 

12350 logger.warning( 

12351 "MCP runtime mode: %s. GET/POST/DELETE /mcp requests will be proxied to %s. MCP session core mode: %s. MCP replay/resume core mode: %s. MCP live stream core mode: %s. MCP affinity core mode: %s. MCP session auth reuse mode: %s.", 

12352 _current_mcp_runtime_mode(), 

12353 settings.experimental_rust_mcp_runtime_uds or settings.experimental_rust_mcp_runtime_url, 

12354 _current_mcp_session_core_mode(), 

12355 _current_mcp_resume_core_mode(), 

12356 _current_mcp_live_stream_core_mode(), 

12357 _current_mcp_affinity_core_mode(), 

12358 _current_mcp_session_auth_reuse_mode(), 

12359 ) 

12360 # First-Party 

12361 from mcpgateway.transports.rust_mcp_runtime_proxy import RustMCPRuntimeProxy # pylint: disable=import-outside-toplevel 

12362 

12363 return RustMCPRuntimeProxy(streamable_http_session.handle_streamable_http) 

12364 

12365 if _rust_build_included(): 

12366 logger.warning( 

12367 "MCP runtime mode: %s. Rust MCP artifacts are present in this image, but EXPERIMENTAL_RUST_MCP_RUNTIME_ENABLED=false so /mcp remains on the Python transport. Set RUST_MCP_MODE=edge or RUST_MCP_MODE=full to activate the Rust runtime with the simple env flow.", 

12368 _current_mcp_runtime_mode(), 

12369 ) 

12370 else: 

12371 logger.info("MCP runtime mode: %s. /mcp is mounted on the Python transport.", _current_mcp_runtime_mode()) 

12372 

12373 return python_transport 

12374 

12375 

12376class InternalTrustedMCPTransportBridge: 

12377 """Trusted internal bridge from Rust MCP transport requests to the Python session manager.""" 

12378 

12379 def __init__(self, transport_app) -> None: 

12380 """Store the underlying Python transport app used for trusted forwarding. 

12381 

12382 Args: 

12383 transport_app: Python transport app that ultimately owns session handling. 

12384 """ 

12385 self.transport_app = transport_app 

12386 

12387 async def handle_streamable_http(self, scope, receive, send): 

12388 """Translate trusted Rust transport requests into Python session-manager calls. 

12389 

12390 Args: 

12391 scope: Incoming ASGI scope. 

12392 receive: ASGI receive callable. 

12393 send: ASGI send callable. 

12394 """ 

12395 if scope.get("type") != "http": 

12396 response = ORJSONResponse(status_code=404, content={"detail": "Not found"}) 

12397 await response(scope, receive, send) 

12398 return 

12399 

12400 method = str(scope.get("method", "GET")).upper() 

12401 if method not in {"GET", "POST", "DELETE"}: 

12402 response = ORJSONResponse(status_code=405, content={"detail": "Method not allowed"}) 

12403 await response(scope, receive, send) 

12404 return 

12405 

12406 request = Request(scope, receive=receive) 

12407 try: 

12408 _build_internal_mcp_forwarded_user(request) 

12409 except HTTPException as exc: 

12410 response = ORJSONResponse(status_code=exc.status_code, content={"detail": exc.detail}) 

12411 await response(scope, receive, send) 

12412 return 

12413 

12414 auth_context = get_internal_mcp_auth_context(request) or {} 

12415 server_id = request.headers.get("x-contextforge-server-id") 

12416 forwarded_scope = dict(scope) 

12417 forwarded_scope["path"] = "/mcp/" 

12418 forwarded_scope["modified_path"] = f"/servers/{server_id}/mcp" if server_id else "/mcp/" 

12419 forwarded_auth_method = auth_context.get("auth_method") or "mcp_internal_forward" 

12420 

12421 token = user_context_var.set(auth_context) 

12422 try: 

12423 set_trace_context_from_teams( 

12424 auth_context.get("teams"), 

12425 user_email=auth_context.get("email"), 

12426 is_admin=bool(auth_context.get("permission_is_admin", auth_context.get("is_admin", False))), 

12427 auth_method=forwarded_auth_method, 

12428 team_name=auth_context.get("team_name"), 

12429 ) 

12430 await self.transport_app.handle_streamable_http(forwarded_scope, receive, send) 

12431 finally: 

12432 user_context_var.reset(token) 

12433 clear_trace_context() 

12434 

12435 

12436mcp_transport_app = _build_mcp_transport_app() 

12437internal_trusted_mcp_transport = InternalTrustedMCPTransportBridge(streamable_http_session) 

12438 

12439# Streamable http Mount 

12440app.mount("/mcp", app=mcp_transport_app.handle_streamable_http) 

12441app.mount("/_internal/mcp/transport", app=internal_trusted_mcp_transport.handle_streamable_http) 

12442 

12443# Conditional static files mounting and root redirect 

12444if UI_ENABLED: 

12445 # Mount static files for UI 

12446 logger.info("Mounting static files - UI enabled") 

12447 try: 

12448 # Create a sub-application for static files that will respect root_path 

12449 static_app = StaticFiles(directory=str(settings.static_dir)) 

12450 STATIC_PATH = "/static" 

12451 

12452 app.mount( 

12453 STATIC_PATH, 

12454 static_app, 

12455 name="static", 

12456 ) 

12457 logger.info("Static assets served from %s at %s", settings.static_dir, STATIC_PATH) 

12458 except RuntimeError as exc: 

12459 logger.warning( 

12460 "Static dir %s not found - Admin UI disabled (%s)", 

12461 settings.static_dir, 

12462 exc, 

12463 ) 

12464 

12465 # Redirect root path to admin UI 

12466 @app.get("/") 

12467 async def root_redirect(): 

12468 """ 

12469 Redirects the root path ("/") to "/admin/". 

12470 

12471 Logs a debug message before redirecting. 

12472 

12473 Returns: 

12474 RedirectResponse: Redirects to /admin/. 

12475 

12476 Raises: 

12477 HTTPException: If there is an error during redirection. 

12478 """ 

12479 logger.debug("Redirecting root path to /admin/") 

12480 root_path = settings.app_root_path 

12481 return RedirectResponse(f"{root_path}/admin/", status_code=303) 

12482 # return RedirectResponse(request.url_for("admin_home")) 

12483 

12484 # Redirect /favicon.ico to /static/favicon.ico for browser compatibility 

12485 @app.get("/favicon.ico", include_in_schema=False) 

12486 async def favicon_redirect() -> RedirectResponse: 

12487 """Redirect /favicon.ico to /static/favicon.ico for browser compatibility. 

12488 

12489 Returns: 

12490 RedirectResponse: 301 redirect to /static/favicon.ico. 

12491 """ 

12492 root_path = settings.app_root_path 

12493 return RedirectResponse(f"{root_path}/static/favicon.ico", status_code=301) 

12494 

12495else: 

12496 # If UI is disabled, provide API info at root 

12497 logger.warning("Static files not mounted - UI disabled via MCPGATEWAY_UI_ENABLED=False") 

12498 

12499 @app.get("/") 

12500 async def root_info(): 

12501 """ 

12502 Returns basic API information at the root path. 

12503 

12504 Logs an info message indicating UI is disabled and provides details 

12505 about the app, including its name, version, and whether the UI and 

12506 admin API are enabled. 

12507 

12508 Returns: 

12509 dict: API info with app name, version, and UI/admin API status. 

12510 """ 

12511 logger.info("UI disabled, serving API info at root path") 

12512 return {"name": settings.app_name, "description": f"{settings.app_name} API"} 

12513 

12514 

12515# Expose some endpoints at the root level as well 

12516app.post("/initialize")(initialize) 

12517app.post("/notifications")(handle_notification) 

« prev     ^ index     » next       coverage.py v7.13.5, created at 2026-05-22 22:58 +0100
